name: Blacephalon

attacks.name: Blazer
attacks.text: Turn 1 of your face-down Prize cards face up. If it's a Fire Energy card, this attack does 50 more damage. (That Prize card remains face up for the rest of the game.)

attacks.name: Fireball Circus
attacks.text: Discard any number of Fire Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 50 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: It slithers toward people. Then, without warning, it triggers the explosion of its own head. It's apparently one kind of Ultra Beast.

name: Zapdos ex
rules: When Pokémon-ex has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Synchronized Lift
abilities.text: As long as you have Articuno ex and Moltres ex in play, the Retreat Cost for Zapdos ex is 0.

attacks.name: Thunder Gift
attacks.text: You may move a Lightning Energy card attached to Zapdos ex to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Lightning Wing
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Arcanine or this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Arcanine does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: A Pokémon that has been long admired for its beauty. It runs gracefully, as if on wings.

name: Flygon

abilities.name: Dragon Guard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to your Dragon Pokémon. (Existing effects are not removed.)

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: This Pokémon hides in the heart of sandstorms it creates and seldom appears where people can see it.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Magnetic Circuit
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

name: Togetic

attacks.name: Chase Up
attacks.text: Search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage done to Togetic during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It grows dispirited if it is not with kind people. It can float in midair without moving its wings.

attacks.name: Plunder
attacks.text: Before doing damage, discard all Trainer cards attached to the Defending Pokémon (before they affect the damage).

name: Xatu

abilities.name: Psychic Shift
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if Xatu is on your Bench, you may choose 1 Special Condition from 1 of your Active Pokémon and remove that Special Condition. Then, 1 of the Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition that you chose.

attacks.name: Energy Singe
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: This odd Pokémon can see both the past and the future. It eyes the sun's movement all day.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Draining Kiss
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Flaaffy

attacks.name: Shock Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: If its coat becomes fully charged with electricity, its tail lights up. It fires hair that zaps on impact.

name: Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frozen Wings
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hail Blizzard
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.

name: Dark Magneton
rules: This Pokémon is both Lightning Darkness type.

attacks.name: Magnetic Lines
attacks.text: If your opponent has at least 2 Pokémon in play, move a basic Energy card from the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Pulse
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Cinderace V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Field Runner
abilities.text: If a Stadium is in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

name: Nidoran ♀

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: While it does not prefer to fight, even one drop of the poison it secretes from its barbs can be fatal.

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

abilities.name: Reactive Healing
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a React Energy card from your hand to Tangela, remove all damage counters from Tangela.

attacks.name: Entangling Vines
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, that Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Gentle Wrap
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Wigglytuff-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rolling Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Lovely Star-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Alolan Geodude

attacks.name: Rock Polish
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: Its body is a magnetic stone. Iron sand attaches firmly to the portions of its body that are particularly magnetic.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Dig Out
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a Fighting Energy card, attach it to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It flies across the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

name: M Glalie-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Cryo Mouth
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has 10 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.

name: Tepig

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It blows fire through its nose. When it catches a cold, the fire becomes pitch-black smoke instead.

name: Golett
flavorText: Ancient science fashioned this Pokémon from clay. It's been active for thousands of years.

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They will cooperate to steal eggs from the nests of bird Pokémon, but fights break out to determine which one gets to eat the eggs.

name: Krabby

attacks.name: Aqua Shower
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

name: Vanillish

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon's habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

name: Unown [J]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Join]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown [J], Unown [O], Unown [I], and Unown Dragon on your Bench, you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

attacks.name: Freeze Lock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach Energy cards from his or her hand to his or her Active Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Crushing Ice
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost. Damage is calculated using the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost after applying effects to the Retreat Cost.

name: Binacle

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

name: Walrein

abilities.name: Gather Ice
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), when you play Walrein from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may attach as many Water Energy cards from your hand to Walrein as you like.

attacks.name: Cold Crush
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Walrein and then discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.

attacks.name: Hug
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

abilities.name: Poison Enzyme
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Budew by your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Roselia from your hand onto Budew (this counts as evolving Budew) and remove all damage counters from Budew.

attacks.name: Buddy-buddy
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Hang High
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: To eat, it deftly shucks nuts with its two tails. It rarely uses its arms now.

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Nutritional Support
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Grass Energy cards and attach them to any of your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hibernation Spore
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Flip 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, the Defending Pokémon is still Asleep.

flavorText: A mushroom grown larger than the host's body controls Parasect. It scatters poisonous spores.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its fin tips leak poison. Its fins and bones are highly valued as ingredients in herbal medicine.

name: Umbreon

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Pursuit
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if the Defending Pokémon tries to retreat, do 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.)

flavorText: When agitated, this Pokémon protects itself by spraying poisonous sweat from its pores.

name: Gyarados

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Dragon Rage
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has an extremely aggressive nature. The Hyper Beam it shoots from its mouth totally incinerates all targets.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Delibird

attacks.name: Present
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for any 1 card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It carries food rolled up in its tail. It has the habit of sharing food with people lost in mountains.

name: Dark Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Dark Song
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Slap Awake
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Asleep or Confused, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage. Then, the Defending Pokémon is no longer Asleep or Confused.

flavorText: This Pokémon has soft, fine hair. When angered, it increases in size and has been known to charge those it considers a threat.

name: Gloom

attacks.name: Strange Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sticky Nectar
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage and, until the end of your next turn, as long as Gloom is your Active Pokémon, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat, and if the effect of an attack, Pokémon Power, or Trainer card would change that player's Active Pokémon, that part of the effect does nothing. If tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: What appears to be drool is actually sweet honey. It is very sticky and clings stubbornly if touched.

attacks.name: Foul Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Misfire
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, put 6 damage counters on Weezing.

name: Slowbro

abilities.name: Strange Behavior
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to Slowbro as long as you don't Knock Out Slowbro. This power can't be used if Slowbro is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The Shellder that is latched onto Slowpoke's tail is said to feed on the host's left-over scraps.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Burn Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard all Fire Energy cards attached to Houndoom.

name: Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Sidestep
attacks.text: If Bonsly is anywhere under Sudowoodo, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Sudowoodo during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Persuade
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Despite appearing to be a tree, its body is closer to rocks and stones. It is very weak to water.

name: Tympole

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pokémon in play that have the Round attack.

flavorText: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans. It uses the rhythm of these sounds to talk.

name: Poliwrath

attacks.name: Focus Punch
attacks.text: If Poliwrath was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Poliwrath does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: With extremely tough muscles, it can keep swimming in the Pacific Ocean without resting.

name: Xerneas

attacks.name: Aurora Gain
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no weakness.

attacks.name: Light of Life
attacks.text: If your opponent has Yveltal (including Yveltal-EX) in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

name: Swanna

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Defog
attacks.text: You may discard any Stadium card in play. If you do, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

name: Galarian Mr. Rime

abilities.name: Shuffle Dance
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent's face-down Prize cards with the top card of their deck. (The cards stay face down.)

attacks.name: Mad Party
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile that has the Mad Party attack.

flavorText: It's highly skilled at tap-dancing. It waves its cane of ice in time with its graceful movements.

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as Granbull is your Active Pokémon, any damage done by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Crushing Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Do the Wave
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The body is soft and rubbery. When angered, it will suck in air and inflate itself to an enormous size.

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Stun Needle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When attacked, they create an electric barrier by spitting out many electrically charged threads.

name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from Piloswine. If it has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

attacks.name: High-Speed Charge
attacks.text: Piloswine does 30 damage to itself. Piloswine can't use this attack during your next turn.

flavorText: If it charges at an enemy, the hairs on its back stand up straight. It is very sensitive to sound.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard 1 Fire Energy card attached to Charmeleon or this attack does nothing.

flavorText: When it swings its burning tail, it raises the temperature to unbearably high levels.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

name: Ampharos-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Power Recharge
attacks.text: Put all Electropower cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Impact Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electrical-GX
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 7 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Budew

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Roselia from your hand onto Budew (this counts as evolving Budew) and remove all damage counters from Budew.

attacks.name: Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Over the winter, it closes its bud and endures the cold. In spring, the bud opens and releases pollen.

name: Quilava

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: Be careful if it turns its back during battle. It means that it will attack with the fire on its back.

name: Clamperl

abilities.name: Evolutionary Advantage
abilities.text: If you go second, this Pokémon can evolve during your first turn.

flavorText: It is protected by a sturdy shell. Once in a lifetime, it makes a magnificent pearl.

name: Heracross

attacks.name: Charging Horn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage.

flavorText: It is normally peaceful, but will attack any who get between it and honey, its favorite food.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Iron Fist of Justice
attacks.text: If you have any Team Plasma Pokémon in play, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Shadow Punch
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Golurk were created to protect people and Pokémon. They run on a mysterious energy.

name: Oshawott

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It fights using the scalchop on its stomach. In response to an attack, it retaliates immediately by slashing.

name: Butterfree

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Butterfree equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10).

flavorText: In battle, it flaps its wings at high speed to release highly toxic dust into the air.

name: Regice

abilities.name: Regi Move
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this power. Discard 2 cards from your hand and choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon that isn't an Evolved Pokémon. Then, your opponent switches that Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Regice is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Ice Reflect
attacks.text: If Regice was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its body is made of ice from the ice age. It controls frigid air of -328 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Bruxish

attacks.name: Gnash Teeth
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Synchronoise
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that shares a type with your opponent's Active Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When it unleashes its psychic power from the protuberance on its head, the grating sound of grinding teeth echoes through the area.

name: Alolan Graveler

attacks.name: Thunder Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Electrobullet
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its preferred food is dravite. After it has eaten this mineral, crystals form inside the Pokémon, rising to the surface of part of its body.

name: Fearow

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Fearow.

flavorText: With its huge and magnificent wings, it can keep aloft without ever having to land for rest.

name: Dark Kadabra

abilities.name: Matter Exchange
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a card from your hand in order to draw a card. This power can't be used if Dark Kadabra is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: Some people say that Kadabras are responsible for many of the mysterious events reported in the news.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Pikachu during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

abilities.name: Burning Aura
abilities.text: As long as Typhlosion is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's) between turns.

attacks.name: Flickering Flames
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Typhlosion.

name: Keldeo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Rush In
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Secret Sword
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Popplio
flavorText: This Pokémon snorts body fluids from its nose, blowing balloons to smash into its foes. It's famous for being a hard worker.

name: Squirtle

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It shelters itself in its shell, then strikes back with spouts of water at every opportunity.

name: Alomomola

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.

name: Plusle

attacks.name: Tag Team Boost
attacks.text: If Minun is on your Bench, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

name: Tapu Bulu-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Nature's Judgment
attacks.text: You may discard all Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Tapu Wilderness-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Strange Barrier
abilities.text: Whenever an attack by a Basic Pokémon (including your own) does 20 or more damage to Erika's Dratini (after applying Weakness and Resistance), reduce that damage to 10. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.) This power stops working while Erika's Dratini is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

name: Greninja

abilities.name: Water Shuriken
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Water Energy card from your hand. If you do, put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mist Slash
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, or any other effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

name: Nidorino

attacks.name: Nido Press
attacks.text: If Nidorina is on your Bench, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

name: Alolan Graveler

attacks.name: Self-Destruct
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its preferred food is dravite. After it has eaten this mineral, crystals form inside the Pokémon, rising to the surface of part of its body.

name: Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Happy Mime
attacks.text: Each player draws a card.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its pantomime skills are wonderful. You may become enraptured while watching it, but next thing you know, Mr. Mime has made a real wall.

attacks.name: Poison Breath
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Donphan

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has sharp, hard tusks and a rugged hide. Its tackle is strong enough to knock down a house.

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Lanturn.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Lanturn does 10 damage to itself.

name: Larvitar

attacks.name: Mountain Munch
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

flavorText: Born deep underground, it comes aboveground and becomes a pupa once it has finished eating the surrounding soil.

name: Tyranitar-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Break Ground
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Cinderace VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Counter
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was damaged by an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does that much more damage.

attacks.name: Max Pyro Ball
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Metal Sound
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

abilities.name: Leap Away
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Toxicroak G is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, return Toxicroak G and all cards attached to it to your hand. This power can't be used if Toxicroak G is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Poison Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Piplup
flavorText: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

name: Luxray

attacks.name: Flash Impact
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It can see clearly through walls to track down its prey and seek its lost young.

name: Binacle

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Raging Thunder
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Pain Split
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on the Defending Pokémon for each damage counter on Misdreavus.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

attacks.name: Cling
attacks.text: After your attack, remove from Gloom the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon. If Gloom has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

attacks.name: Double Razor Leaf
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Breloom

attacks.name: Drain Punch
attacks.text: Remove from Breloom a number of damage counters equal to the amount of Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Homing Uppercut
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost is 0, this attack does 60 damage plus 60 more damage.

flavorText: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

attacks.name: Reverse Thrust
attacks.text: Switch Horsea with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Toxicroak

abilities.name: Poison Sacs
abilities.text: Your opponent can't remove the Special Condition Poisoned by evolving or devolving his or her Poisoned Pokémon. (This also includes putting a Pokémon Level-Up card onto the Poisoned Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Knuckle Claws
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Jellyfish Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The tentacles are normally kept short. On hunts, they are extended to ensnare and immobilize prey.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It has small electric sacs on both its cheeks. If threatened, it looses electric charges from the sacs.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Houndoom.

attacks.name: Dark Impact
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't use any Poké-Powers until the end of your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Hawlucha

attacks.name: Midair Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Although its body is small, its proficient fighting skills enable it to keep up with big bruisers like Machamp and Hariyama.

abilities.name: Self-healing
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Fire Energy card from your hand to Flareon, remove all Special Conditions affecting Flareon.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Burn Booster
attacks.text: Discard an Energy card attached to Flareon in order to use this attack. If the discarded card is a Fire Energy card, this attack does 40 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Lapras

abilities.name: Mermaid's Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put a Misty's Favor card from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It likes swimming around with people on its back. In the Alola region, it's an important means of transportation over water.

name: Granbull

attacks.name: Raging Charge
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Granbull. Then, Granbull does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is actually timid and easily spooked. If attacked, it flails about to fend off its attacker.

name: Unown [A]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Anger]
abilities.text: Whenever 1 of your Pokémon with Unown in its name uses its Hidden Power attack, that attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on Unown [A]. If you have more than 1 Unown [A] in play, use only 1 [Anger] for each attack.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Zamazenta

abilities.name: Sturdy Shield
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its ability to deflect any attack led to it being known as the Fighting Master's Shield. It was feared and respected by all.

name: Cacturne

attacks.name: Spike Wound
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 50 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Poison Experiment
attacks.text: You may discard a Grass or Darkness Energy attached to Cacturne. If you discard a Grass Energy, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If you discard a Darkness Energy, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day's desert heat.

name: Altaria

abilities.name: Fight Song
abilities.text: Your Dragon Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

abilities.name: Duplicate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for another Ditto and switch it with Ditto. (Any cards attached to Ditto, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Ditto on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Duplicate Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: After your attack, you may switch Ditto with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Crimson Bull
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Tauros is now Confused.

flavorText: They fight each other by locking horns. The herd's protector takes pride in its battle-scarred horns.

name: Frillish
flavorText: If its veil-like arms stun and wrap a foe, that foe will be dragged miles below the surface, never to return.

abilities.name: Psymimic
abilities.text: Once during your turn, instead of Alakazam's normal attack, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon's attacks. Alakazam copies that attack including its Energy costs and anything else required in order to use that attack, such as discarding Energy cards. (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Alakazam's type is still Psychic.) This power can't be used if Alakazam is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Syncroblast
attacks.text: If Alakazam and the Defending Pokémon don't have the same number of Energy cards attached to them, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 80.

name: Victory Cup
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Chewtle

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: Apparently the itch of its teething impels it to snap its jaws at anything in front of it.

name: Wash Rotom

abilities.name: Roto Motor
abilities.text: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon's attacks.

attacks.name: Wash Arrow
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

name: Unown

abilities.name: DARK
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for a Darkness Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

abilities.name: Energy Guard
abilities.text: As long as Shelgon has any basic Energy cards attached to it, damage done to Shelgon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Miltank

attacks.name: Moomoo Squeeze
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Moomoo Milk card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If it is around babies, the milk it produces contains much more nutrition than usual.

name: Suicune

attacks.name: Aurora Loop
attacks.text: Put 2 Water Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: Said to be the embodiment of north winds, it can instantly purify filthy, murky water.

name: Weavile

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: One Weavile will trip a Sandshrew and flip it over, and then another Weavile will deal the finishing blow with its sharp claws.

name: Gulpin

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It has a small heart and brain. Its stomach compromises most of its body, with enzymes to dissolve anything.

name: Helioptile
flavorText: The frills on either side of its head have cells that generate electricity when exposed to sunlight.

name: Krabby

attacks.name: Super Slice
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It can be found near the sea. The large pincers grow back if they are torn out of their sockets.

abilities.name: Vigorous Aura
abilities.text: As long as Medicham is your Active Pokémon, attacks by each player's Active Pokémon do 10 more damage to any Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rolling Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rend
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage. If not, this attack does 20 damage.

name: Corphish

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Its hardy vitality enables it to adapt to any environment. Its pincers will never release prey.

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it raises all the barbs on its body. These barbs grow slower than Nidorino's.

name: Kirlia
flavorText: The cheerful spirit of its Trainer gives it energy for its psychokinetic power. It spins and dances when happy.

attacks.name: Delta Circle
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Pokémon you have in play that has δ on its card.

attacks.name: Split Bomb
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Attack from Behind
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Confused, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.

name: Fearow

attacks.name: Fury Attack
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It has the stamina to fly all day on its broad wings. It fights by using its sharp beak.

name: Seaking

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage. If tails, this attack does 10 damage.

attacks.name: Horn Swipe
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage. If 1 or both of them are tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: Using its horn, it bores holes in riverbed boulders, making nests to prevent its eggs from washing away.

name: Double Aqua Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to Team Aqua Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of the turn you attached it.
This card provides WaterWater Energy only while it is attached to a Team Aqua Pokémon.
(If this card is attached to anything other than a Team Aqua Pokémon, discard this card.)

name: Plusle

attacks.name: Positive Hand
attacks.text: Discard a basic Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Electric Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It cheers on friends with pom-poms made of sparks. It drains power from telephone poles.

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Dark One-eye
attacks.text: You may discard a card from your hand. If you do, your opponent discards a card from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its body is hollow. It is said that those who look into its body are sucked into the void.

name: Ducklett

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: These bird Pokémon are excellent divers. They swim around in the water eating their favorite food—peat moss.

attacks.name: Self Charge
attacks.text: Attach a Darkness Energy card from your hand to Nuzleaf.

abilities.name: Reactive Shield
abilities.text: As long as Tentacruel has any React Energy cards attached to it, prevent all effects, including damage, done to any of your Tentacruel in play by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon-ex.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Fury Strikes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Ultra Energy Source
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of basic Energy cards attached to all of the Active Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Mischief
attacks.text: Shuffle your opponent's deck.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Top Drop
attacks.text: Discard the top card from your opponent's deck. If you discarded a Pokémon, this attack does damage equal to the HP of that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Brick Break
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

name: Dark Pupitar
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

attacks.name: Explosive Evolution
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.) Then, search your deck for a card that evolves from Dark Pupitar and put it on Dark Pupitar. (This counts as evolving Dark Pupitar.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Double Tackle
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sniff Out
attacks.text: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Negative Spark
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Bodies. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

name: Abra

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Using its ability to read minds, it will identify impending danger and teleport to safety.

name: Crushing Hammer
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Palpitoad

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It lives in the water and on land. It uses its long, sticky tongue to immobilize its opponents.

name: Thundurus

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Disaster Volt
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The spikes on its tail discharge immense bolts of lightning. It flies around the Unova region firing off lightning bolts.

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Vaporeon

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Vaporeon.

attacks.name: Dual Splash
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: When Vaporeon's fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Although unattractive and unpopular, this Pokémon's marvelous vitality has made it a subject of research.

name: Floatzel

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Floatzel during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Aqua Jet
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It floats using its well-developed floatation sac. It assists in the rescues of drowning people.

name: Jellicent

abilities.name: Stickiness
abilities.text: The Retreat Cost of each of your opponent's Pokémon in play is Colorless more.

attacks.name: Eerie Light
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its body is mostly seawater. It's said there's a castle of ships Jellicent have sunk on the seafloor.

name: Typhlosion

abilities.name: Blazing Energy
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may use this Ability. All Energy attached to your Pokémon are Fire Energy instead of their usual type until the end of your turn. (This includes cards that come into play on this turn.)

attacks.name: Lost Flame
attacks.text: Put 2 Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It has a secret, devastating move. It rubs its blazing fur together to cause huge explosions.

name: Minun

attacks.name: Negative Discard
attacks.text: Put 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Exposure to electricity from Minun and Plusle promotes blood circulation and relaxes muscles.

name: Moltres & Zapdos & Articuno-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sky Legends-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck. If this Pokémon has at least 1 extra Fire, Water, and Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 110 damage to 3 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Magnetic Reversal
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Nosepass is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch. This power can't be used if Nosepass is affected by a Special Condition.

name: Feebas

attacks.name: Float On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It is the shabbiest Pokémon of all. It forms in schools and lives at the bottom of rivers.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If Kirlia and the Defending Pokémon have a different amount of Energy attached to them, this attack's base damage is 30 instead of 60.

attacks.name: Power Whip
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage for each Energy attached to Carnivine G to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Grass Knot
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).

name: Magnemite
flavorText: The electromagnetic waves emitted by the units at the sides of its head expel antigravity, which allows it to float.

name: Manectric
flavorText: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Energize
attacks.text: Attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spark Bolt
attacks.text: You may discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Its electric charges can reach even 100,000 volts. Careless contact can cause even an Indian elephant to faint.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Regigigas

abilities.name: Recover Mechanism
abilities.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to Regigigas, remove all Special Conditions from Regigigas.

attacks.name: Gigaton Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 60 damage plus 20 more damage and does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: There is an enduring legend that states this Pokémon towed continents with ropes.

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard a Psychic Energy attached to Alakazam E4 and remove 4 damage counters from Alakazam E4.

attacks.name: Mysterious Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spinning Blow
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If both coins are heads, switch Forretress with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Scatterbomb
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, do 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. For each tails, do 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: It has a calm and caring nature. Because its horn grows slowly, it prefers not to fight.

name: Seismitoad

attacks.name: Siphon Off
attacks.text: Attach 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Shaky Fall
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused. That Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They shoot paralyzing liquid from their head bumps. They use vibration to hurt their opponents.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Bi-Blast
attacks.text: If Vibrava has any React Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Flaaffy

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: As a result of storing too much electricity, it developed patches where even downy wool won't grow.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Air Crash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wings of Destruction
attacks.text: If your opponent has Xerneas (including Xerneas-EX) in play, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: When this legendary Pokémon's wings and tail feathers spread wide and glow red, it absorbs the life force of living creatures.

name: Weezing

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Weezing does 60 damage to itself.

flavorText: Where two kinds of poison gases meet, two Koffings can fuse into a Weezing over many years.

name: Victory Cup
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Swalot

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.text: If, before doing damage, your opponent's Active Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It swallows anything whole. It sweats toxic fluids from its follicles to douse foes.

abilities.name: Energy Protection
abilities.text: Any damage done to Metapod by attacks is reduced by 10 for each Energy attached to Metapod. You can't reduce more than 30 damage in this way.

name: Malamar

attacks.name: Eerie Wave
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Random Peck
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar's control.

name: Absol

abilities.name: Cursed Eyes
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may move 3 damage counters from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of his or her Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: It appears when it senses an impending natural disaster. As a result, it was mistaken as a doom bringer.

name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Alolan Golem-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Super Electromagnetic Tackle
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Heavy Rock-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any cards from their hand during their next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Sonic Scream
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Sleep Drain
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. Remove 4 damage counters from Parasect.

flavorText: The larger the mushroom on its back grows, the stronger the mushroom spores it scatters.

attacks.name: Acid
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to Roserade C, remove all Special Conditions from Roserade C.

attacks.name: Magical Leaf
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage and remove 2 damage counters from Roserade C.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coin. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Blitzle

attacks.name: Delivery Dash
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Electropower cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

name: Brionne

attacks.name: Disarming Voice
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A skillful dancer, it creates a sequence of water balloons as it dances, and briskly bombards it enemies.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Shuppet and put it onto Shuppet. (This counts as evolving Shuppet.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Machop
flavorText: Though small in stature, it is powerful enough to easily heft and throw a number of Geodude at once.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Thrash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

name: Dark Machoke

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. Do the damage to the new Defending Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: While normally peaceful and friendly, the number of incidents involving cruel Machokes is rising.

name: M Tyranitar-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: θ Double
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Destroyer King
attacks.text: This attack does 60 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Decidueye

abilities.name: Deep Forest Camo
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon V and Pokémon-GX.

attacks.name: Splitting Arrow
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye's shots never miss.

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Skill Dive
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Even though it has no eyes, it can sense obstacles using ultrasonic waves it emits from it mouth.

name: Blitzle
flavorText: When thunderclouds cover the sky, it will appear. It can catch lightning with its mane and store the electricity.

name: Alomomola
flavorText: Floating in the open sea is how they live. When they find a wounded Pokémon, they embrace it and bring it to shore.

name: Wailmer
flavorText: It swims along with its mouth open and swallows down seawater along with its food. It sprays excess water out of its nostrils.

abilities.name: Sleep Pendulum
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Hypno is your Active Pokémon, you may make the Defending Pokémon Asleep. This power can't be used if Hypno is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Spiral Aura
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon isn't Knocked Out by the damage from this attack, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch the Defending Pokémon with it.

name: Growlithe
flavorText: Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

name: Dark Dragonair

abilities.name: Evolutionary Light
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for an Evolution card. Show it to your opponent and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Dark Dragonair is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: As is fitting for a legendary Pokémon, it has many strange powers, including the ability to make rain or snow fall.

attacks.name: Stretch Vine
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Carnivine.

attacks.name: Vine Extract
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

name: Manectric

attacks.name: Energy Assist
attacks.text: Attach 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

name: Wartortle

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is a well-established symbol of longevity. If its shell has algae on it, that Wartortle is very old.

name: Tapu Koko-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Aero Trail
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may move any number of Lightning Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tapu Thunder-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Grotle

attacks.name: Planting
attacks.text: Attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The shell is hardened soil. Some Pokémon come to peck the berries growing on the trees on its back.

name: Kingler

attacks.name: Heavy Pincers
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Claw Rend
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

attacks.name: Plasma
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Electabuzz.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Tynamo
flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

name: Frost Rotom

abilities.name: Roto Motor
abilities.text: If you have 9 or more Pokémon Tool cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in the attack cost of each of this Pokémon's attacks.

attacks.name: Frost Crush
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

abilities.name: Energy Connect
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may take a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Benched Pokémon and attach it to your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Ampharos is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Lightning Strike
attacks.text: You may discard all Lightning Energy cards attached to Ampharos. If you do, this attack's base damage is 80 instead of 40.

name: Lanturn

abilities.name: Hydroelectric Power
abilities.text: You may make Floodlight do 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Lanturn, but not used to pay for Floodlight's Energy cost. This power can't be used if Lanturn is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Floodlight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The light it emits is so bright that it can illuminate the sea's surface from a depth of over three miles.

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.text: When you attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to Grovyle, remove all Special Conditions from Grovyle.

name: Huntail

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dangerous Bite
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It lives deep in the sea. With a tail shaped like a small fish, it attracts unsuspecting prey.

name: Cascoon

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if Cascoon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to Cascoon if that damage is 30 or less.

attacks.name: Gooey Thread
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It is hot inside its cocoon. All the cells in its body create the energy for it to evolve.

name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Volt Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stimulates its muscles with electricity, boosting the strength in its legs and enabling it to run 100 yards in five seconds.

name: Tapu Fini-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Ring
attacks.text: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Shot
attacks.text: Discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 120 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Tapu Storm-GX
attacks.text: Shuffle your opponent's Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it into their deck. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Nidoqueen

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its entire body is armored with hard scales. It will protect the young in its burrow with its life.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.text: Damage done to Shelgon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance) during your opponent's next turn.

name: Dark Pupitar
rules: This Pokémon is both Fighting Darkness type.

attacks.name: Dark Streak
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

name: Bulbasaur

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Bulbasaur.

flavorText: A strange seed was planted on its back at birth. Thus, a plant sprouted and now grows with this Pokémon.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: One-Two Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Heel Drop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has a perfect sense of balance and can lash out with powerful kicks from any position.

abilities.name: Clear Body
abilities.text: Metang can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

attacks.name: Extra Comet Punch
attacks.text: During your next turn, Extra Comet Punch does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Ursaring

attacks.name: Headpress
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).

attacks.name: Double Lariat
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Although it is a good climber, it prefers to snap trees with its forelegs and eat fallen berries.

name: Double Magma Energy
rules: This card can only be attached to Team Magma Pokémon. Discard this card at the end of the turn you attached it.
This card provides FightingFighting Energy only while it is attached to a Team Magma Pokémon.
(If this card is attached to anything other than a Team Magma Pokémon, discard this card.)

abilities.name: Dual Armor
abilities.text: As long as Magcargo has any Fighting Energy attached to it, Magcargo is both Fire and Fighting type.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Extra Flame
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 40 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Thundering Hurricane
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A legendary Pokémon that is said to live in thunderclouds. It freely controls lightning bolts.

name: Swanna

attacks.name: Healing Dance
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Incessant Peck
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

name: Diancie

attacks.name: Sparkling Wish
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Diamond Storm
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from each of your Fairy Pokémon.

flavorText: A sudden transformation of Carbink, its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

name: Wartortle

abilities.name: Solid Shell
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Aqua Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: When tapped, this Pokémon will pull in its head, but its tail will still stick out a little bit.

name: Drednaw

attacks.name: Vise Wave
attacks.text: If you played Nessa from your hand during this turn, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.

name: Dodrio

abilities.name: Retreat Aid
abilities.text: As long as Dodrio is Benched, pay Colorless less to retreat your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Dodrio.

flavorText: Uses its three brains to execute complex plans. While two heads sleep, one head stays awake.

name: Pokémon Center
rules: Remove all damage counters from all of your own Pokémon with damage counters on them, then discard all Energy cards attached to those Pokémon.

name: Galarian Mr. Mime

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Triple Spin
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its talent is tap-dancing. It can also manipulate temperatures to create a floor of ice, which this Pokémon can kick up to use as a barrier.

name: Charmeleon

attacks.name: Fire Fang
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Flare Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a Fire Energy attached to Charmeleon and this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: In the rocky mountains where CHARMELEON live, their fiery tails shine at night like stars.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It raises its tail to check its surroundings. The tail is sometimes struck by lightning in this pose.

name: Altaria

abilities.name: Fight Song
abilities.text: Your Dragon Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Iron Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Stantler

attacks.name: Terrorize
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, choose 1 of its attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Overhead Toss
attacks.text: If you have any Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to 1 of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Those who stare at its antlers will gradually lose control of their senses and be unable to stand.

name: Snorunt
flavorText: It feeds mainly on ice and snow. It's only able to survive in a limited number of places in the warm Alola region.

name: Blitzle

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use their flashing manes to communicate with one another.

name: Unown [I]

abilities.name: ITEM
