attacks.name: Inviting Bell
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It emits cries by agitating an orb at the back of its throat. It moves with flouncing hops.

abilities.name: Primal Aura
abilities.text: As long as Kabutops is your Active Pokémon, neither player can play Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from his or her hand to evolve Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dual Cut
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

name: Mr. Mime

abilities.name: Bench Barrier
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It shapes an invisible wall in midair by minutely vibrating its fingertips to stop molecules in the air.

name: Espurr

attacks.name: Energy Teaser
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to another of their Pokémon.

flavorText: It has enough psychic energy to blast everything within 300 feet of itself, but it has no control over its power.

name: Greninja BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Greninja BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

abilities.name: Giant Water Shuriken
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may discard a Water Energy card from your hand. If you do, put 6 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Electroweb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They employ an electrically charged web to trap their prey. While it is immobilized by shock, they leisurely consume it.

name: Grotle

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from Grotle.

flavorText: It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.

name: Shedinja

abilities.name: Empty Shell
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: A discarded bug shell that came to life. Peering into the crack on its back is said to steal one's spirit.

name: Lairon

attacks.name: Heavy Metal
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Metal Energy attached to Lairon. This attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Lairon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: For food, it digs up iron ore. It smashes its steely body against others to fight over territory.

name: Mismagius

attacks.name: Chaos Wheel
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon Tool, Special Energy, or Stadium cards from their hand during their next turn.

attacks.name: Dark Arts
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each card in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It appears as if from nowhere—muttering incantations, placing curses, and giving people terrifying visions.

name: Uxie

abilities.name: Set Up
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Uxie from your hand onto your Bench, you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Psychic Restore
attacks.text: You may put Uxie and all cards attached to it on the bottom of your deck in any order.

flavorText: Known as "The Being of Knowledge." It is said that it can wipe out the memory of those who see its eyes.

abilities.name: Core Guard
abilities.text: As long as Starmie has any Psychic Energy attached to it, damage done to Starmie by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Energy Flip
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) You may move an Energy attached to that Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Freeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack. (Benching or evolving the Defending Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon; if tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Because the long hair all over its body obscures its sight, it just keeps charging repeatedly.

name: Gastly

attacks.name: Sleeping Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Destiny Bond
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Gastly in order to use this attack. If a Pokémon Knocks Out Gastly during your opponent's next turn, Knock Out that Pokémon.

flavorText: Almost invisible, this gaseous Pokémon cloaks the target and puts it to sleep without notice.

name: Pumpkaboo

attacks.name: Night March
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of Pokémon in your discard pile that have the Night March attack.

flavorText: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

abilities.name: Burning Energy
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may turn all basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon into Fire Energy for the rest of the turn. This power can't be used if Charizard is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Scorching Whirlwind
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If 1 of them is tails, discard 2 Energy cards attached to Charizard. If both are tails, discard all Energy cards attached to Charizard.

name: Unown

abilities.name: CURE
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

name: Kecleon

abilities.name: Camouflage
abilities.text: If any basic Energy card attached to Kecleon is the same as the Attacking Pokémon's type, any damage done by attacks from the Pokémon to Kecleon is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Blind Scratch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does no damage to the Defending Pokémon. Instead, this attack does 20 damage to 1 of your Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can freely change its body's color. The zigzag pattern on its belly doesn't change, however.

name: Quilava

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to Quilava.

flavorText: This Pokémon is fully covered by nonflammable fur. It can withstand any kind of fire attack.

name: Heliolisk

attacks.name: Random Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Volt Switch
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Lightning Pokémon.

flavorText: It stimulates its muscles with electricity, boosting the strength in its legs and enabling it to run 100 yards in five seconds.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Yamask

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves in past ages. Each retains memories of its former life.

abilities.name: Extra-tight
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to Shuckle by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon-ex.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

name: Dark Wartortle

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Mirror Shell
attacks.text: If an attack does damage to Dark Wartortle during your opponent's next turn (even if Dark Wartortle is Knocked Out), Dark Wartortle attacks the Defending Pokémon for an equal amount of damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a negative attitude, it hides in its shell whenever it's in a bad mood—which is often.

name: Electrode-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Extra Energy Bomb
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach 5 Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon, except Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, in any way you like. If you do, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Crush and Burn-GX
attacks.text: Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 50 more damage for each card you discarded in this way. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Skorupi

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It grips prey with its tail claws and injects poison. It tenaciously hangs on until the poison takes.

attacks.name: Seek Out
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Basculin

attacks.name: Swarming Bites
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Basculin you have in play to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Savage, violent Pokémon, red and blue Basculin are always fighting each other over territory.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Whimsy Tackle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its long tail often breaks off. It doesn't really feel any pain, though, and the tail grows back, so Slowpoke isn't particularly bothered.

abilities.name: Shock Blast
abilities.text: If Lt. Surge's Electrode is your Active Pokémon and gets damaged (even if it's Knocked Out), flip a coin. If tails, this power does 20 damage to each Active Pokémon. This power works even if Lt. Surge's Electrode is already Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed when it takes damage.

attacks.name: Power Ball
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

name: Rotom

attacks.name: Electribonus
attacks.text: Discard a Lightning Energy card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Poltergeist
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. This attack does 20 damage times the number of Trainer cards in your opponent's hand.

flavorText: Its electric-like body can enter some kinds of machines and take control in order to make mischief.

name: Milotic V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Aqua Impact
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Hypno Splash
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Jynx

abilities.name: Victory Kiss
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may heal 10 damage from your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hug
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It wiggles its hips as it walks. It can cause people to dance in unison with it.

name: Lapras VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: G-Max Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Tentacool

attacks.name: Bubble Jutsu
attacks.text: If you played Janine from your hand during this turn, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It drifts through the sea searching for prey. Its poisonous tentacles break off sometimes, but after a while, they grow back.

abilities.name: Magnetic Flow
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Magneton is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon that have Energy cards attached to them. Choose 1 of the Energy cards attached to each of those Pokémon and switch them between the Pokémon. This power can't be used if Magneton is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Steel Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that are the same type (color) as the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Krabby

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Krabby and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench if full.)

flavorText: Its pincers are not only powerful weapons, they are used for balance when walking sideways.

name: Archen
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Plume Fossil

abilities.name: Prehistoric Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is in your discard pile, you may put this Pokémon on the bottom of your deck.

flavorText: It was revived from an ancient fossil. Not able to fly, it lived in treetops and hopped from one branch to another.

name: Zekrom-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Glinting Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Strong Volt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Tropical Wind
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 2 damage counters from each Active Pokémon (remove 1 damage counter if a Pokémon has only 1). If tails, each Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Lucario
flavorText: By reading the auras of all things, it can tell how others are feeling from over half a mile away.

name: Mudkip
flavorText: To alert it, the fin on its head senses the flow of water. It has the strength to heft boulders.

name: Wormadam Sandy Cloak

attacks.name: Cloak Headbutt
attacks.text: If you have Wormadam Trash Cloak in play, this attack does 60 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: When evolving, its body takes in surrounding materials. As a result, there are many body variations.

name: Espeon

attacks.name: Psy Alert
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Shadow Ball
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Also apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its fur is so sensitive, it can feel minute shifts in the air and predict the weather…and its foes' thoughts.

name: Electabuzz

attacks.name: Lightning Rod
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and put a Lightning Rod marker on it. (A Pokémon can have only 1 Lightning Rod marker on it at a time.)

attacks.name: Lightning Bolt
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to any Pokémon with a Lightning Rod marker on it. Apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: Normally found near power plants, it can wander away and cause major blackouts in cities.

name: Primarina-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bubble Beat
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Roaring Seas
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grand Echo-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from all of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Lairon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Shelgon does 10 damage to itself.

name: Metagross

abilities.name: Magnetic Warp
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Iron Cannon
attacks.text: You may discard all Metal Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

name: Togekiss

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

abilities.name: Serene Grace
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 8 cards of your deck. Choose any basic Energy cards you find there and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.

flavorText: It shares many blessings with people who respect one another's rights and avoid needless strife.

name: Snorlax
flavorText: It doesn't do anything other than eat and sleep. When prompted to make a serious effort, though, it apparently displays awesome power.

name: Unown

abilities.name: RETURN
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, you may return all Energy attached to 1 of your Pokémon to your hand.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat until the end of your opponent's next turn.

name: Skuntank

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It sprays a vile-smelling fluid from the tip of its tail to attack. Its range is over 160 feet.

attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Remove 3 damage counters from Kirlia.

attacks.name: Psychic Boom
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dark Aid
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for Pokémon Tool cards and Rocket's Secret Machine cards. You may show either 1 Pokémon Tool card or Rocket's Secret Machine card to your opponent and put it into your hand, or show a combination of 3 Pokémon Tool cards or Rocket's Secret Machine cards to your opponent and shuffle them into your deck.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Hoodwink
attacks.text: You may search your opponent's discard pile for up to 3 in any combination of Trainer, Supporter, or Stadium cards and put them into your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It can slip through any obstacle. It lurks inside walls to keep an eye on its foes.

name: Lickitung

attacks.name: Tongue Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tongue can be extended like a chameleon's. It leaves a stinging sensation when it licks enemies.

name: Shedinja

abilities.name: Marvel Shell
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Shedinja by your opponent's Pokémon that has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies.

attacks.name: Spike Wound
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A discarded bug shell that came to life. Peering into the crack on its back is said to steal one's spirit.

name: Araquanid

abilities.name: Water Bubble
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Fire Pokémon.

flavorText: It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

attacks.name: Lick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Cranidos

attacks.name: Steamroll
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lived in jungles around 100 million years ago. Its skull is as hard as iron.

name: Kyurem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frozen Wings
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hail Blizzard
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Hail Blizzard during your next turn.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: It looks just like a Poké Ball. It is dangerous because it may electrocute or explode on contact.

name: Persian δ
rules: This Pokémon is both Darkness Metal type.

attacks.name: Scratch and Draw
attacks.text: If any Stadium card with Holon in its name is in play, draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Deceive
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 1 of his or her Pokémon. Put 4 damage counters on that Pokémon.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Aqua Shock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has any Water Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: While one alone doesn't have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

name: Light Jolteon

attacks.name: Pulse Guard
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or more damage is done to Light Jolteon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Thunder Needle
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads. If you get 2 or more heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It can collect the ambient electrical energy in its cells and expel it in massive bursts.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Trans Tail
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Lightning Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It makes electricity with pouches in its cheeks and shoots charges from its tail. It lives atop trees.

name: Eevee
flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The female's horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

name: Muk

attacks.name: Gentle Wrap
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Toxic Secretion
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: A toxic fluids seeps from its body. The fluid instantly kills plants and trees on contact.

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

name: Igglybuff
rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

abilities.name: Gaze
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that has a Pokémon Power. That power stops working until the end of this turn. This effect ends if that Pokémon leaves the Bench.

flavorText: It has a very soft body. If it starts to roll, it will bounce all over and be impossible to stop.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dream Eater
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It can slip through any obstacle. It lurks inside walls to keep an eye on its foes.

name: Zapdos

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

attacks.name: Thunderbolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: One of the legendary bird Pokémon. While it is flying, it makes crackling and snapping sounds.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bolt Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: Concealing itself in lightning clouds, it flies throughout the Unova region. It creates electricity in its tail.

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Power Heal
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Medicham. After doing damage, remove 1 damage counter from Medicham.

name: Hoopa

attacks.name: Hyperspace Punch
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Portal Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Portal Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.

name: Pidove

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: These Pokémon live in cities. They are accustomed to people. Flocks often gather in parks and plazas.

name: Mewtwo-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Burst
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Super Absorption
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psystrike-GX
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Flareon

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Once it has stored up enough heat, this Pokémon's body temperature can reach up to 1,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Luxio

attacks.name: Disconnect
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

name: Sealeo
flavorText: It has a very sensitive nose. It touches new things with its nose to examine them.

name: Joltik
flavorText: They attach themselves to large-bodied Pokémon and absorb static electricity, which they store in an electric pouch.

name: Linoone

attacks.name: Switcheroo
attacks.text: Move a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon (excluding Pokémon that already has a Pokémon Tool attached to it). (If an effect of this attack is prevented, this attack does nothing.)

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It charges prey at speeds over 60 mph. However, because it can only run straight, it often fails.

name: Arbok

attacks.name: Gastro Acid
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon has no Abilities until the end of your next turn.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The pattern on its belly appears to be a frightening face. Weak foes will flee just at the sight of the pattern.

name: Vanillite

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: This Pokémon formed from icicles bathed in energy from the morning sun. It sleeps buried in snow.

name: Clamperl

attacks.name: Shell Protection
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 50 or less.

flavorText: It is protected by a sturdy shell. Once in a lifetime, it makes a magnificent pearl.

name: Voltorb
flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

attacks.name: Flytrap
attacks.text: Before doing damage, choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with his or her Active Pokémon. This attack can't be used if your opponent has no Benched Pokémon.

name: Galarian Darmanitan

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Crushing Headbutt
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Crushing Headbutt.

flavorText: On days when blizzards blow through, it comes down to where people live. It stashes food in the snowball on its head, taking it home for later.

name: Sigilyph

attacks.name: Quick Turn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Psychic Assault
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They never vary the route they fly, because their memories of guarding an ancient city remain steadfast.

abilities.name: Strikes Back
abilities.text: If Vigoroth is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Vigoroth is Knocked Out), put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

attacks.name: Pollen Shield
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, Ledian can't become affected by a Special Condition. (Any other effects of attacks, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, and Trainer cards still happen.)

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Geodude
flavorText: It uses its arms to steadily climb steep mountain paths. It swings its fists around if angered.

name: Full Heal
rules: Remove all Special Conditions from your Active Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Graveler (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: Rolls down slopes to move. It rolls over any obstacle without slowing or changing its direction.

name: Chinchou
flavorText: It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.

attacks.name: Toxic Grip
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Spoink
flavorText: It bounces constantly, using its tail like a spring. The shock of bouncing keeps its heart beating.

attacks.name: Influence
attacks.text: Search your deck for Omanyte, Kabuto, Aerodactyl, Lileep, or Anorith and put up to 2 of them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. Treat the new Benched Pokémon as Basic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Time Spiral
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Evolved Pokémon in play, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, remove the highest Stage Evolution card on that Pokémon and have your opponent shuffle it into his or her deck.

name: Pyukumuku

abilities.name: Innards Out
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It lives in shallow seas, such as areas near a beach. It can eject its internal organs, which it uses to engulf its prey or battle enemies.

name: Archeops

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Swift Dive
attacks.text: If this Pokémon's remaining HP is 50 or less, this attack's base damage is 50.

flavorText: It runs better than it flies. It takes off into the sky by running at a speed of 25 mph.

name: Electrode

abilities.name: Buzzap Generator
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may search your deck for up to 2 Lightning Energy cards and attach them to your Lightning Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. If you searched your deck in this way, this Pokémon is Knocked Out.

flavorText: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

name: Joltik

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: Since it can't generate its own electricity, it sticks onto large-bodied Pokémon and absorbs static electricity.

name: Huntail

attacks.name: Powerful Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It lives deep in the sea. With a tail shaped like a small fish, it attracts unsuspecting prey.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Psychic Research
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Supporter card and use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack. (The Supporter card remains in your discard pile.)

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: It is highly perceptive of its Trainer's feelings. It dances when it is feeling happy.

name: Wormadam Trash Cloak

attacks.name: Cloak Shard
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. If you have Wormadam Plant Cloak in play, this attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon instead. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Serve Trash
attacks.text: You may search your opponent's discard pile for any 1 card, show it to your opponent, and put it on top of his or her deck.

flavorText: When evolving, its body takes in surrounding materials. As a result, there are many body variations.

name: Drifloon

attacks.name: Sneaky Placement
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a "Signpost for Wandering Spirits."

name: Victreebel

attacks.name: Burning Scent
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned. Before applying these effects, you may switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Energy Dissolve
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon at the end of your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It pools in its mouth a fluid with a honeylike scent, which is really an acid that dissolves anything.

name: Metagross

ancientTrait.name: θ Double
ancientTrait.text: This Pokémon may have up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards attached to it.

attacks.name: Machine Gun Stomp
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each card in your hand.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Metang combined to form it. With four brains, it has the intelligence of a supercomputer.

name: Chinchou

attacks.name: Spark
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives in the depths beyond the reach of sunlight. It flashes lights on its antennae to communicate with others of its kind.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Metal Ball
attacks.text: Put 5 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Energy Stream
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a basic Energy card and attach it to Poliwhirl.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Growth
attacks.text: Attach a Grass Energy card from your hand to Bulbasaur.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Gigashock
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon (1 if there is only 1). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Brick Break
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Machoke's boundless power is very dangerous, so it wears a belt that suppresses its energy.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Hypnotic Gaze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

name: Sandile

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It moves along below the sand's surface, except for its nose and eyes. A dark membrane shields its eyes from the sun.

name: Meowstic

attacks.name: Teleportation Burst
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The eyeball patterns on the interior of its ears emit psychic energy. It keeps the patterns tightly covered because that power is too immense.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.

name: Light Machoke

attacks.name: Return Home
attacks.text: If you have any Benched Pokémon, shuffle 1 of them and all cards attached to it into your deck.

attacks.name: Gentle Kick
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has no damage counters on it, this attack does 40 damage. If it has any, this attack does 20 damage.

flavorText: This Pokémon never gets tired, no matter what it does or how hard it works.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psyslash
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew's genes. It's said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

name: Spoink
flavorText: It bounces constantly, using its tail like a spring. The shock of bouncing keeps its heart beating.

name: Luxray

abilities.name: Intimidating Fang
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks do 30 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Volt Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

name: Kyurem

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Frost Spear
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It generates a powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Wicked Tentacles
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another of their Pokémon. If you do, put 3 damage counters on the Pokémon you moved the Energy to.

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It fires off ultrasonic waves from its red orbs to weaken its prey, and then it wraps them up in its 80 tentacles.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Flop attack's base damage is 50.

flavorText: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

name: Cofagrigus

attacks.name: Damagriiigus
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Perplex
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

abilities.name: Immunity
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks done to Machamp.

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Before doing damage, if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and switch it with the Defending Pokémon. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, ignore this effect.

attacks.name: Hurricane Punch
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Quagsire

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Due to its relaxed and carefree attitude, it often bumps its head on boulders and boat hulls as it swims.

name: Zekrom

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bolt Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 40 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon appears in legends. In its tail, it has a giant generator that creates electricity.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Geodude does 50 damage to itself.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Tickle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its irregularly configured DNA is affected by its surroundings. It evolves if its environment changes.

name: Meowstic-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Shadow Ear
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Dialga

attacks.name: Time Call
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from 1 of your Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Time Wager
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack's base damage is 50 instead of 100.

flavorText: It has the power to control time. It appears in Sinnoh-region myths as an ancient deity.

name: Croconaw

abilities.name: Evolutionary Vitality
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you play Croconaw from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose all Energy cards you find there, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hover Over
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Once it bites down, it won't let go until it loses its fangs. New fangs quickly grow into place.

name: Heatran

attacks.name: Lava Burn
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Heat Bazooka
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Lt. Surge's Raticate's Double-edge attack's damage (base damage and damage to itself) is doubled.

attacks.name: Double-edge
attacks.text: Lt. Surge's Raticate does 20 damage to itself.

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It lives in arid places. Instead of perspiration, it expels grainy sand from its body.

name: Noibat

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: They live in pitch black caves. Their enormous ears can emit ultrasonic waves of 200,000 hertz.

name: Qwilfish

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

name: Jangmo-o

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o lives.

name: Walrein

attacks.name: Cold Snap
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from their hand during their next turn. If 1 of your Pokémon used Cold Snap during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It shatters ice with its big tusks. Its thick blubber repels not only the cold, but also enemy attacks.

name: Eelektrik

attacks.name: Small Appetite
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon's maximum HP is 100 or more, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It wraps itself around its prey and paralyzes it with electricity from the round spots on its sides. Then it chomps.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Shadow Cage
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

name: Goldeen

attacks.name: Fin Smack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its dorsal, pectoral and tail fins wave elegantly in the water. That is why it is known as the water dancer.

name: Omanyte

abilities.name: Clairvoyance
abilities.text: Your opponent plays with his or her hand face up. This power stops working while Omanyte is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Omanyte but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

flavorText: Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically resurrected from fossils.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Flickering Tail
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Shining Mew

attacks.name: Legendary Guidance
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Because it can use all kinds of moves, many scientists believe Mew to be the ancestor of Pokémon.

name: Electrode
flavorText: It is known to drift on winds if it is bloated to bursting with stored electricity.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Tumble Over
attacks.text: Riolu can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

name: Gourgeist

abilities.name: Gourgantic
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Grass Energy attached to it, its maximum HP is 200.

attacks.name: Horror Note
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of cards in your hand.

flavorText: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

name: Electabuzz
flavorText: It loves to feed on strong electricity. It occasionally appears around large power plants and so on.

name: Growlithe
flavorText: Very protective of its territory. It will bark and bite to repel intruders from its space.

name: Hawlucha

attacks.name: Backflip
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Wicked Jab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Eevee, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Tirtouga
flavorText: It was restored from an ancient fossil. Tirtouga lived in the sea but came up onto the land to search for prey.

name: Mew

abilities.name: Neutral Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Mew by Evolved Pokémon. You can't use this power if Mew is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

name: Solrock

abilities.name: Sunshine Fate
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Lunatone in play, you may look at the top 3 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order. This power can't be used if Solrock is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Luna Turn
attacks.text: If Lunatone is in your discard pile, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage. Then, search your discard pile for Lunatone, show it to your opponent, and shuffle it into your deck.

flavorText: A new Pokémon species, rumored to be from the sun. It gives off light while spinning.

name: Tympole

attacks.name: Spiral Attack
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Graceful ripples running across the water's surface are a sure sign that Tympole are singing in high-pitched voices below.

abilities.name: Submerge
abilities.text: As long as Whiscash is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Whiscash by your opponent's attacks.

attacks.name: Magnitude
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Audino

attacks.name: Double Slap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It touches others with the feelers on its ears, using the sound of their heartbeats to tell how they are feeling.

name: Horsea

attacks.name: Fin Smack
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its big, developed fins move rapidly, allowing it to swim backward while still facing forward.

abilities.name: Heavyweight
abilities.text: As long as there is a Grass Energy attached to Muk, you must pay an additional ColorlessColorless to retreat it.

attacks.name: Burning Sludge
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Grass Energy attached to Muk. If any of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned and Burned.

name: Bill's Analysis
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal up to 2 Trainer cards you find there and put them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Slowbro

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Facade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is Burned or Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It spaces out while gazing at the sea. With Shellder's poison flowing through its body, it becomes even spacier.

abilities.name: Makeover
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a basic Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex). If you do, search your discard pile for a basic Energy card (excluding the one you discarded) and attach it to that Pokémon. This power can't be used if Smeargle is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Split Spiral Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Great Ball
rules: Look at the top 7 cards of your deck. You may reveal a Pokémon you find there and put it into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 50 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machoke.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Machoke does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Aqua Trick
attacks.text: Move 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If your opponent has no Benched Pokémon, this effect does nothing.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Rapid Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Evolution card named Gyarados or Dark Gyarados and put it on Magikarp. (This counts as evolving Magikarp.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Recent research has revealed the presence of dragon DNA in this species of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Secrete Poison
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever your opponent's attack damages Kakuna (even if Kakuna is Knocked Out), your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned and Kakuna does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Although it is a cocoon, it can move a little. It can extend its poison barb if it is attacked.

name: Tapu Koko ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

abilities.name: Dance of the Ancients
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may choose 2 of your Benched Pokémon and attach a Lightning Energy card from your discard pile to each of them. If you do, discard all cards from this Pokémon and put it in the Lost Zone.

flavorText: It confuses its enemies by flying too quickly for the eye to follow. It has a hair-trigger temper but forgets what made it angry an instant later.

name: Kingdra

ancientTrait.name: α Growth
ancientTrait.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.

attacks.name: Gather Strength
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 4 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Dragon Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Water and a Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It stores energy by sleeping at underwater depths at which no other life-forms can survive.

name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Wash Over
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its wings deliver wicked blows that snap even the thickest of trees. It searches for prey in icy seas.

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

abilities.name: Energy Refresh
abilities.text: As long as Leafeon is your Active Pokémon, whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, remove 2 damage counters from that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Plus Energy
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Vanillish

attacks.name: Ice Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Snowy mountains are this Pokémon's habitat. During an ancient ice age, they moved to southern areas.

attacks.name: Dig Deep
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand.

name: Zekrom-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Glinting Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Strong Volt
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Black Cry
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat or use any Poké-Powers during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dark Slash
attacks.text: You may discard a Darkness Energy attached to Houndoom G. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Nidoran ♂

attacks.name: Double Stab
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Its large ears are flapped like wings when it is listening to distant sounds. It extends toxic barbs when angered.

name: Loudred

attacks.name: Bass Control
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: The shock waves from its cries can tip over trucks. It stamps its feet to power up.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Foul Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned; if tails, it is now Confused.

flavorText: Because it stores several kinds of toxic gases in its body, it is prone to exploding without warning.

name: Unown

abilities.name: FLASH
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Unown from your hand onto your Bench, you may look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: Its flat, thin body is always stuck on walls. Its shape appears to have some meaning.

name: Dewgong

abilities.name: Cold Fat
abilities.text: As long as Dewgong is Asleep, any damage done to Dewgong by attacks is reduced by 40 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Collapse
attacks.text: Dewgong is now Asleep.

flavorText: In snow, the pure white coat covering its body obscures it from predators.

name: Haunter

abilities.name: Transparency
abilities.text: Whenever an attack does anything to Haunter, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Haunter. This power stops working while Haunter is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Because of its ability to slip through block walls, it is said to be from another dimension.

name: Palpitoad

attacks.name: Twirling Sign
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It weakens its prey with sound waves intense enough to cause headaches, then entangles them with its sticky tongue.

name: White Kyurem-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Blade
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Dragon Nova-GX
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Paralyzed. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Blaine's Last Stand
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Draw 2 cards for each Fire Pokémon you have in play.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Ditto

abilities.name: Metamorphosis Gene
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, it can use the attacks of your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

attacks.name: Stick On
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has the ability to reconstitute its entire cellular structure to transform into whatever it sees.

attacks.name: Mid-air Crush
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent discards 1 Energy card, if any, attached to that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nosedive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Swellow does 10 damage to itself.

name: Lombre

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Supporter cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Absorb
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Lombre.

flavorText: It has a mischievous spirit. If it spots an angler, it will tug on the fishing line to interfere.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card named Murkrow and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

attacks.name: Flock Attack
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Murkrows on your Bench. This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

name: Pachirisu

attacks.name: Snuggly Generator
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Nuzzle attack, search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

name: Articuno

abilities.name: Aurora Veil
abilities.text: As long as Articuno is your Active Pokémon, your Benched Pokémon do not take damage from and are not affected by attacks. This power cannot be used if Articuno is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A legendary bird Pokémon that is said to appear to doomed people who are lost in icy mountains.

name: Honedge

abilities.name: Final Hour
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, put 3 damage counters on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: If anyone dares to grab its hilt, it wraps a blue cloth around that person's arm and drains that person's life energy completely.

name: Porygon

attacks.name: Conversion 1
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has a Weakness, you may change it to a type of your choice other than Colorless.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: More and more people play the slots every day, trying to win a Porygon.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Rock Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It usually clings to cliffs. When it spots its prey, it spreads its wings and glides down to attack.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Bagon.

name: Mantine

attacks.name: Water Reserve
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Water Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: If it builds up enough speed swimming, it can jump out above the waves and glide for over 300 feet.

name: Metapod

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 20 or less damage is done to Metapod (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen).

attacks.name: Hatch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove all damage counters from Metapod. Then, search your deck for a card that evolves from Metapod and attach that card to Metapod. This counts as evolving Metapod. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Inside the shell, it is soft and weak as it prepares to evolve. It stays motionless in the shell.

name: Gligar

attacks.name: Stun Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: It flies straight at its target's face then clamps down on the startled victim to inject poison.

name: Grumpig

attacks.name: Tricky Steps
attacks.text: You may move an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does a strange dance to control foes' minds.

name: Jolteon

abilities.name: Thunderous Awakening
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, Water Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities.

flavorText: If it is angered or startled, the fur all over its body bristles like sharp needles that pierce foes.

name: Wailmer

attacks.name: Double Attack
attacks.text: Choose 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It bounces playfully like a ball. The more seawater it swallows, the higher it bounces.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: X Ball
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psydrive
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Vanilluxe

attacks.name: Frigid Breath
attacks.text: Until the end of your next turn, each player can't play any Supporter or Stadium cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Deep Freeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Swallowing large amounts of water, they make snow clouds inside their bodies and, when angry, cause violent blizzards.

name: Electivire
flavorText: It pushes the tips of its two tails against the foe, then lets loose with over 20,000 volts of power.

name: Bruxish

attacks.name: Vivid Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psychic Fangs
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It stuns its prey with psychokinesis and then grinds them to mush with its strong teeth. Even Shellder's shell is no match for it.

name: Vanillite

attacks.name: Ice Over
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Unable to survive in hot areas, it makes itself comfortable by breathing out air cold enough to cause snow. It burrows into the snow to sleep.

attacks.name: Eruption
attacks.text: Each player discard the top card of his or her deck. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each Fire Energy card discarded in this way.

attacks.name: Fire Stream
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Magcargo in order to use this attack. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, this attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Houndour

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It is smart enough to hunt in packs. It uses a variety of cries for communicating with others.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from Grimer and put it onto Grimer. (This counts as evolving Grimer.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: X Ball
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psydrive
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Oranguru

abilities.name: Instruct
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw cards until you have 3 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

name: Seviper

attacks.name: Poison Buildup
attacks.text: Seviper is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Poison Effect
attacks.text: If Seviper is Poisoned, this attack does 20 damage plus 60 more damage and remove the Special Condition Poisoned from Seviper.

flavorText: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

abilities.name: Moonlight
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put a card from your hand back on your deck. If you do, search your deck for a basic Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Clefable is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 times the number of heads.

name: Krokorok

attacks.name: Dark Clamp
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Double Swing
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They live in groups of a few individuals. Protective membranes shield their eyes from sandstorms.

name: Gorebyss

attacks.name: Captivate
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its swimming form is exquisitely elegant. With its thin mouth, it feeds on seaweed that grows between rocks.

name: Arceus
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.

attacks.name: Judgment
attacks.text: Discard all Energy cards attached to Arceus and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to have emerged from an egg in a place where there was nothing, then shaped the world.

name: Cofagrigus

abilities.name: Durable Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: They pretend to be elaborate coffins to teach lessons to grave robbers. Their bodies are covered in pure gold.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

abilities.name: Earth Rage
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Nidoking is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, put a damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This power can't be used if Nidoking is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Giant Horn
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Latios

attacks.name: Break Through
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A highly intelligent Pokémon. By folding back its wings in flight, it can overtake jet planes.

name: Galarian Sirfetch'd V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Resolute Spear
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when this Pokémon moves from your Bench to the Active Spot, you may move any amount of Fighting Energy from your other Pokémon to it.

attacks.name: Meteor Smash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't attack.

name: Drifloon
flavorText: Because of the way it floats aimlessly, an old folktale calls it a "Signpost for Wandering Spirits."

name: Beldum

attacks.name: Calculate
attacks.text: Look at the top 4 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: Its cells are all magnetic, and it communicates with others by using magnetic pulses.

name: Zeraora

attacks.name: Wild Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It electrifies its claws and tears its opponents apart with them. Even if they dodge its attack, they'll be electrocuted by the flying sparks.

abilities.name: Buffer
abilities.text: If Skiploom would be Knocked Out by an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, Skiploom is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

attacks.name: Miracle Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Special Condition. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

name: Registeel

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Forbidden Iron Hammer
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-EX, discard an Energy attached to that Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is said to be harder than any kind of metal. A study has revealed that its body is hollow.

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage times the number of Energy cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Signal Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It creates barriers from electrified silk that stun foes. This works as a weapon as well as a defense.

attacks.name: Telekinesis
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Fortunate Eye
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your opponent's deck and put them back on top of his or her deck in any order.

flavorText: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Magnetic Resonance
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Magnetic Release
attacks.text: Does 40 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It is actually three Magnemite linked by magnetism. A group can set off a magnetic storm.

attacks.name: Cross-Cut
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is an Evolved Pokémon, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Brush Off
