attacks.text: Search your deck for a Water Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: A friendly Pokémon that captures the subtle flows of seawater using its two antennae.

attacks.name: Self Charge
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your hand to Team Aqua's Electrike.

attacks.name: Invigorate
attacks.text: Choose 1 Basic Pokémon in any player's discard pile. Put it onto that player's Bench. Put a number of damage counters on that Pokémon equal to half its HP (rounded down to the nearest 10). (You can't put a Pokémon on a Bench that's full.)

attacks.name: Pendulum Curse
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Pupitar

attacks.name: Dust Devil
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each non-Fighting Pokémon in play. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: Its shell is as hard as sheet rock, and it is also very strong. Its thrashing can topple a mountain.

name: Arceus LV.X
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.
Put this card onto your Active Arceus. Arceus LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Multitype
abilities.text: Arceus LV. X's type is the same type as its previous Level.

abilities.name: Omniscient
abilities.text: Arceus can use the attacks of all Arceus you have in play as its own. (You still need the necessary Energy to use each attack.)

name: Castform

abilities.name: Temperament
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform. (Any cards attached to Castform, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) If you do, put Castform on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward. You can't use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Weather Ball
attacks.text: If you have a Stadium card in play, remove 3 damage counters from Castform. If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Wobbuffet

attacks.name: Shadowy Knot
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It hates light and shock. If attacked, it inflates its body to build up its counterstrike.

attacks.name: Magnitude
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Abra

attacks.name: Vanish
attacks.text: Shuffle Abra into your deck. (Discard all cards attached to Abra.)

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It teleports itself away from danger, making it difficult to capture.

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damages times the number of heads.

name: Carbink BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Carbink BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Diamond Gift
attacks.text: Attach 2 Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Fighting Pokémon.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Ram
attacks.text: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is knocked out.)

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

name: Scolipede

attacks.name: Random Peck
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Poison Ring
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. That Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It clasps its prey with the claws on its neck until it stops moving. Then it finishes it off with deadly poison.

name: Golett

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

name: Drampa

attacks.name: Dragon Wisdom
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Dragon Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is friendly to people and loves children most of all. It comes from deep in the mountains to play with children it likes in town.

name: Doublade

attacks.name: Curse of the Blade
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on each of your opponent's Pokémon that has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it.

flavorText: The complex attack patterns of its two swords are unstoppable, even for an opponent greatly accomplished at swordplay.

name: Tentacool

abilities.name: Cowardice
abilities.text: At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may return Tentacool to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to Tentacool.) This power can't be used the turn you put Tentacool into play or if Tentacool is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: Drifts in shallow seas. Anglers who hook them by accident are often punished by its stinging acid.

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The female's horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

name: Piloswine

attacks.name: Rouse
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Piloswine. Then, remove 4 damage counters from Piloswine.

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its shaggy coat makes it unable to see. It checks surroundings with its sensitive nose instead.

name: Inteleon V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Snipe Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Aqua Report
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand.

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Lookout
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aura Break
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Darkness or Fairy Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It's hypothesized that it's monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.

name: Beginning Door
rules: Search your deck for Arceus, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Gigalith

abilities.name: High Density Armor
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP, any damage done to this Pokémon by an opponent's attack is reduced by 50 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Overdrive Smash
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Overdrive Smash attack does 40 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The solar rays it absorbs are processed in its energy core and fired as a ball of light.

name: Diancie-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Moonblast
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 20 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Luminous Swirl
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

name: Milo
rules: Discard up to 2 cards from your hand, and draw 2 cards for each card you discarded in this way.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Dragonite

abilities.name: Max Wind
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may heal all damage from 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mach Press
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Fury Cutter
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage if exactly 1 is heads, or 20 more damage if exactly 2 are heads, or 40 more damage if exactly 3 are heads, or 80 more damage if all 4 are heads.

flavorText: It slashes through grass with its sharp scythes, moving too fast for the human eye to track.

attacks.name: Rapids
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of those Energy cards and discard it.

attacks.name: Water Punch
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the number of Water Energy attached to Misty's Poliwhirl. This attack does 30 damage plus 10 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Phione

abilities.name: Whirlpool Suction
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if this Pokémon is on your Bench, you may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon. If you do, discard all cards attached to this Pokémon and put it on the bottom of your deck.

flavorText: When the water warms, they inflate the flotation sac on their heads and drift languidly on the sea in packs.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Minun

attacks.name: Ditch and Draw
attacks.text: You may discard any number of cards from your hand. Then, draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.

flavorText: It cheers on friends. If its friends are losing, its body lets off more and more sparks.

name: Haunter

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Dream Eater
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is not Asleep, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: If you get the feeling of being watched in darkness when nobody is around, Haunter is there.

name: Tapu Lele

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magical Swap
attacks.text: Move any number of damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon to their other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This guardian deity of Akala is guilelessly cruel. The fragrant aroma of flowers is the source of its energy.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Magmar from your hand onto Magby (this counts as evolving Magby) and remove all damage counters from Magby.

attacks.name: Ignite
attacks.text: If Full Flame is in play, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Yamask

attacks.name: Transfer Pain
attacks.text: Move 1 damage counter from any of your Pokémon to any of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves. Each retains memories of its former life.

name: Castform Rain Form

abilities.name: Temperament
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform Rain Form. (Any cards attached to Castform Rain Form, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform Rain Form back into your deck. You can't use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.text: The defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

name: Jangmo-o

attacks.name: Rigidify
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: They live in mountains where no trace of humans can be detected. Jangmo-o grow little by little as they battle one another.

name: Phantump

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

flavorText: These Pokémon are created when spirits possess rotten tree stumps. They prefer to live in abandoned forests.

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Dark Mind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Clefairy

attacks.name: Minimize
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Clefairy by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: The moonlight that it stores in the wings on its back apparently gives it the ability to float in midair.

name: Aegislash

attacks.name: Ticking Knock Out
attacks.text: During your next turn, if the Defending Pokémon is damaged by an attack, it will be Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Draining Blade
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to become king.

name: Nidoqueen

abilities.name: Queen's Call
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Pokémon that isn't a Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Power Lariat
attacks.text: This attack does 50 more damage for each Evolution Pokémon on your Bench.

flavorText: It uses its scaly, rugged body to seal the entrance of its nest and protect its young from predators.

attacks.name: Clutch
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Supersonic Flight
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Lairon

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Lairon fight over territory, and when their steel bodies collide, sparks fly.

name: Seviper

abilities.name: More Poison
abilities.text: Put 1 more damage counter on your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

name: Durant

attacks.name: Chip Off
attacks.text: Discard cards from your opponent's hand at random until he or she has 4 cards in his or her hand.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Individuals each play different roles in driving Heatmor, their natural predator, away from their colony.

name: Houndour
flavorText: It uses different kinds of cries for communicating with others of its kind and for pursuing its prey.

name: Monferno

attacks.name: Flame Dance
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Fire Energy card and attach it to Monferno.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: To intimidate attackers, it stretches the fire on its tail to make itself appear bigger.

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch the Defending Pokémon with it. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Rapture Pollen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep. If tails, remove 2 damage counters from Parasect.

attacks.name: Jump Over
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them. Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Deleting Glare
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

abilities.name: Energy Shark
abilities.text: If Sharpedo is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Sharpedo is Knocked Out), flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: If an attack does damage to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does 40 more damage to that Pokémon until the end of your next turn.

name: Zubat

abilities.name: Free Flight
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has no Energy attached to it, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It does not need eyes, because it emits ultrasonic waves to check its surrounding while it flies.

name: Woobat
flavorText: Its habitat is dark forests and caves. It emits ultrasonic waves from its nose to learn about its surroundings.

name: Sudowoodo

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Sudowoodo.

attacks.name: Wood Hammer
attacks.text: Sudowoodo does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: It stands along paths pretending to be a tree. If it starts raining, it seems to disappear.

abilities.name: Cold Feet
abilities.text: If Mightyena is affected by a Special Condition, ignore all Energy necessary to use Mightyena's attacks.

attacks.name: Collude
attacks.text: If you played any Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Desperate Attack
attacks.text: If Mightyena has less Energy attached to it than the Defending Pokémon, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Eelektrik

attacks.name: Shock Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

name: Anorith
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Claw Fossil Anorith

abilities.name: Restored Barrier
abilities.text: Each of your Restored Pokémon has no Weakness.

attacks.name: X-Scissor
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: A Pokémon ancestor that was reanimated from a fossil. It lived in the sea and hunted with claws.

name: Blitzle
flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

name: Pumpkaboo

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

name: Garbodor

attacks.name: Trashalanche
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Item card in your opponent's discard pile.

attacks.name: Acid Spray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Beware the poisonous liquid it shoots from its right arm. If even a little of it gets on you, you'll experience the effects of the unidentified toxin.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

attacks.name: Aqua Crush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Blown Kiss
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Their ribbonlike feelers increase their psychic power. They are always staring at something.

name: Dewgong

attacks.name: Dual Blizzard
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage to 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It swims through the ocean at a speed of eight knots, searching for Pokémon that will become its prey. It's especially fond of Wishiwashi.

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Araquanid

attacks.name: Tangle Drag
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sticky Web
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks cost Colorless more, and its Retreat Cost is Colorless more.

flavorText: It usually passes its time in the water. When its belly is full, it stores its subdued prey in the water bubble on its head.

name: Machoke
flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

name: Blacephalon-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Bursting Burn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned and Confused.

attacks.name: Mind Blown
attacks.text: Put any amount of Fire Energy attached to your Pokémon in the Lost Zone. This attack does 50 damage for each card put in the Lost Zone in this way.

attacks.name: Burst-GX
attacks.text: Discard 1 of your Prize cards. If it's an Energy card, attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Pupitar

abilities.name: Energy Protection
abilities.text: Any damage done to Pupitar by attacks is reduced by 10 for each Energy attached to Pupitar (after applying Weakness and Resistance). You can't reduce more than 30 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Rock Smash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its body is hard as bedrock. By venting pressurized gas, it can launch itself like a rocket.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Cyndaquil.

attacks.name: Minor Errand-Running
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Lullaby
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Sonic Wing
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

name: Oricorio

attacks.name: Zappy Pom-Poms
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It creates an electric charge by rubbing its feathers together. It dances over to its enemies and delivers shocking electrical punches.

name: Nidorino

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: An aggressive Pokémon that is quick to attack. The horn on its head secretes a powerful venom.

name: Nuzleaf

attacks.name: Dirty Trick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The sound of its grass flute makes its listeners uneasy. It lives deep in forests.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

name: Sigilyph

abilities.name: Safeguard
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: The guardians of an ancient city, they use their psychic power to attack enemies that invade their territory.

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

abilities.name: Backup
abilities.text: Once during each of your turns (before your attack), if you have 2 or fewer cards in your hand, you may draw cards from your deck until you have 3 cards in your hand. This power can't be used if Porygon2 is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hypnotic Ray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Triple Smash
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Leech Life
attacks.text: Remove a number of damage counters from Zubat equal to the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If Zubat has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

flavorText: Forms colonies in perpetually dark places. Uses ultrasonic waves to identify and approach targets.

abilities.name: Aqua Recycle
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your discard pile for a Water Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. This power can't be used if Starmie is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Synchro Gain
attacks.text: If Starmie and the Defending Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, remove 4 damage counters from Starmie.

attacks.name: Powerful Spin
attacks.text: Starmie can't attack during your next turn.

name: Yamask
flavorText: These Pokémon arose from the spirits of people interred in graves. Each retains memories of its former life.

name: Nidoran ♂
flavorText: It scans its surroundings by raising its ears out of the grass. Its toxic horn is for protection.

name: Mismagius

attacks.name: Upper Hand
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its cry sounds like an incantation. It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.

abilities.name: Power Diffusion
abilities.text: As long as Rhydon is your Active Pokémon, prevent all damage done by attacks to all of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hyper Tail
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Poké-Powers or Poké-Bodies, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Granite Head
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Bagon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Flabébé

attacks.name: Aromatherapy
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from each of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It draws out and controls the hidden power of flowers. The flower Flabébé holds is most likely part of its body.

name: Cottonee

attacks.name: Cotton Bed
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Perhaps because they feel more at ease in a group, they stick to others they find. They end up looking like a cloud.

name: Charjabug

attacks.name: Shocking Jaws
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: From the food it digests, it generates electricity, and it stores this energy in its electric sac.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A descendant of the legendary animal baku, which is said to eat dreams. It is skilled at hypnotism.

attacks.name: Bounce Off
attacks.text: If Magneton and the Defending Pokémon don't have the same number of Energy cards attached to them, the player controlling the Active Pokémon with the fewest number of Energy cards attached to it switches 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon with his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magnetic Wave
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 damage times the number of your Benched Pokémon minus the number of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (For example, if your opponent has 1 Benched Pokémon and you have 3, this attack will do 30 damage plus 20 more damage.)

name: Seaking
flavorText: In the autumn spawning season, they can be seen swimming powerfully up rivers and creeks.

name: Spiritomb

attacks.name: Lightless World
attacks.text: Put 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Terrify
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

name: Burmy Plant Cloak

abilities.name: Cloak Evolution
abilities.text: Burmy Plant Cloak can evolve during the turn you play it.

flavorText: Even if it is born where there are no cocooning materials, it somehow always ends up with a cloak.

name: Unown [Q]

abilities.name: QUICK
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Unown Q is on your Bench, you may discard all cards attached to Unown Q and attach Unown Q to 1 of your Pokémon as a Pokémon Tool card. As long as Unown Q is attached to a Pokémon, you pay Colorless less to retreat that Pokémon.

flavorText: Shaped like ancient writing, it is a huge mystery whether language or Unown came first.

attacks.name: Selfdestruct
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Baltoy does 50 damage to itself.

name: Solosis
flavorText: They drive away attackers by unleashing psychic power. They can use telepathy to talk with others.

name: M Diancie-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Diamond Force
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to each of your Pokémon from your opponent's Pokémon-EX. (If this Pokémon is no longer your Active Pokémon, this effect ends.)

name: Tornadus

attacks.name: Energy Wheel
attacks.text: Move an Energy from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hurricane
attacks.text: Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The lower half of its body is wrapped in a cloud of energy. It zooms through the sky at 200 mph.

name: Munna
flavorText: It eats the dreams of people and Pokémon. When it eats a pleasant dream, it expels pink-colored mist.

name: Light Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Evolution Song
attacks.text: Your opponent may choose 1 of his or her Pokémon and search his or her deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon. Your opponent attaches that card to that Pokémon. This counts as evolving that Pokémon. Either way, you may do the same, and then each player who searched shuffles his or her deck.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If two of them brush against each other, they will not be able to separate themselves, as they enjoy touching each other's soft fur too much.

name: Swoobat

attacks.name: Attract
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It emits sound waves of various frequencies from its nose, including some powerful enough to destroy rocks.

name: Primal Kyogre-EX
rules: Primal Reversion rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes Primal Kyogre-EX, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

ancientTrait.name: α Growth
ancientTrait.text: When you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon (except with an attack, Ability, or Trainer card), you may attach 2 Energy cards.

attacks.name: Tidal Storm
attacks.text: Move 2 Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon-EX. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Gourgeist

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Pumpkin Bomb
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

name: Armaldo

attacks.name: Rushing Water
attacks.text: Move an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Guard Claw
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, its body is very sturdy. It skewers prey with its claws to feed.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Wartortle but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Smash Turn
attacks.text: After your attack, you may switch Wartortle with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

name: Charmander

abilities.name: Gather Fire
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may take 1 Fire Energy card attached to 1 of your other Pokémon and attach it to Charmander. This power can't be used if Charmander is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: Many people find it cute and try to hold it, only to be burned by its tail.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Wreck
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 60 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

flavorText: It is said that Golurk were ordered to protect people and Pokémon by the ancient people who made them.

name: Castform Snow-Cloud Form

abilities.name: Temperament
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform Snow-cloud Form. (Any cards attached to Castform Snow-cloud Form, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform Snow-cloud Form back into your deck. You can't use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Hail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

name: Raichu

attacks.name: Thunderclap Shot
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon-EX. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Electrosmash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It can loose 100,000-volt bursts of electricity instantly downing foes several times its size.

attacks.name: Helping Hand
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Remove any number of damage counters from that Pokémon, then draw that many cards.

attacks.name: Hug
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Gothitelle

abilities.name: Magic Room
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Item cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Madkinesis
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Psychic Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

attacks.name: Sacred Wing
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 60.

name: Piers
rules: Search your deck for an Energy card and a Darkness Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Seadra

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Mud Splash
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: An examination of its cells revealed the presence of a gene not found in Horsea. It became a hot topic.

name: Cubchoo
flavorText: When this Pokémon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn't like.

name: Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Fireworks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Cyndaquil.

flavorText: It is timid, and always curls itself up in a ball. If attacked, it flares up its back for protection.

attacks.name: Magnetic Swirl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Toxic Grip
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Seviper

abilities.name: More Poison
abilities.text: Put 1 more damage counter on your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Venomous Fang
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: In battle, it uses its bladed tail to counter any Zangoose. It secretes a deadly venom in its tail.

name: Golisopod-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.

attacks.name: Armor Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Crossing Cut-GX
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Trevenant

abilities.name: Forest's Curse
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play any Item cards from his or her hand.

attacks.name: Tree Slam
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It can control trees at will. It will trap people who harm the forest, so they can never leave.

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It likes playing mischievous tricks such as screaming and wailing to startle people at night.

attacks.name: Call for Friend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, you may search your deck for a Basic Pokémon with Misty in its name and put it onto your Bench. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Ultimate Zone
rules: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.
During each player's turn, the player may move an Energy card attached to 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon to his or her Active Arceus as often as he or she likes.

name: Qwilfish

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Needle Storm
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads you get.

flavorText: To fire its poison spikes, it must inflate its body by drinking over 2.6 gallons of water all at once.

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Pidgeotto

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage.

flavorText: It flies over its wide territory in search of prey, downing it with its highly developed claws.

name: Eelektross

attacks.name: Suction Heal
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Slurp Shakedown
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage to the new Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: With their sucker mouths, they suck in prey. Then they use their fangs to shock the prey with electricity.

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Luxray BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Luxray BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Wild Fury
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Onix (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

flavorText: As it grows, the stone portions of its body harden to become similar to a diamond, though colored black.

name: Mr. Mime-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Magic Evens
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by your opponent's attacks if that damage is exactly 20, 40, 60, 80, 100, 120, 140, 160, 180, 200, 220, 240, or 260.

attacks.name: Breakdown
attacks.text: For each card in your opponent's hand, put 1 damage counter on their Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Life Trick-GX
attacks.text: Heal all damage from this Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Pebble Throw
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Flabébé
flavorText: It draws out and controls the hidden power of flowers. The flower Flabébé holds is most likely part of its body.

name: Raichu BREAK
rules: BREAK Evolution Rule: Raichu BREAK retains the attacks, Abilities, Weakness, Resistance, and Retreat Cost of its previous Evolution.

attacks.name: Grand Bolt
attacks.text: Discard all Energy attached to this Pokémon.

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Rumble
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Geostrike
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It's hypothesized that it's monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.

attacks.name: Super Icy Wind
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Landorus

attacks.name: Abundant Harvest
attacks.text: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gaia Hammer
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Lands visited by Landorus grant such bountiful crops that it has been hailed as "The Guardian of the Fields."

name: Salandit

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It burns its bodily fluids to create a poisonous gas. When its enemies become disoriented from inhaling the gas, it attacks them.

name: Pelipper

attacks.name: Guzzle
attacks.text: Choose up to 2 basic Water Energy cards from your hand and attach them to Pelipper. Remove 2 damage counters for each Energy card attached in this way.

attacks.name: Jet Return
attacks.text: Does 30 times the number of Energy cards attached to Pelipper. Then, put all Energy cards attached to Pelipper on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It dips its large bill in the sea, then scoops up numerous prey along with water.

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to Shelgon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Zebstrika

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: When this ill-tempered Pokémon runs wild, it shoots lightning from its mane in all directions.

name: Cyndaquil
flavorText: It usually stays hunched over. If it is angry or surprised, it shoots flames out of its back.

name: Golbat

attacks.name: Spiral Drain
attacks.text: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Flitting around in the dead of night, it sinks its fangs into its prey and drains a nearly fatal amount of blood.

name: Nidorino
flavorText: It raises its big ears to check its surroundings. If it senses anything, it attacks immediately.

name: Drednaw

attacks.name: Vise Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

name: Mr. Fuji
rules: Choose a Pokémon on your Bench. Shuffle it and any cards attached to it into your deck.

name: Brock's Training
rules: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Geodude, Graveler, Golem, Onix-GX, Cubone, Rhyhorn, Rhydon, or Sudowoodo.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Trubbish

attacks.name: Poison Gas
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Inhaling the gas they belch will make you sleep for a week. They prefer unsanitary places.

name: Musharna

abilities.name: Forewarn
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and put it into your hand. Put the other card back on top of your deck.

attacks.name: Fluffy Dream
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: With the mist from its forehead, it can create shapes of things from dreams it has eaten.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Foul Gas
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its thin, balloon-like body is inflated by horribly toxic gases. It reeks when it is nearby.

name: Houndour

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To corner prey, they check each other's location using barks that only they can understand.

name: Marshadow

abilities.name: Let Loose
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may have each player shuffle their hand into their deck and draw 4 cards.

attacks.name: Shadow Punch
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Able to conceal itself in shadows, it never appears before humans, so its very existence was the stuff of myth.

name: Burmy Sandy Cloak

abilities.name: Cloak Evolution
abilities.text: Burmy Sandy Cloak can evolve during the turn you play it.

flavorText: Even if it is born where there are no cocooning materials, it somehow always ends up with a cloak.

name: Eevee
flavorText: It has the ability to alter the composition of its body to suit its surrounding environment.

name: Castform Sunny Form

abilities.name: Temperament
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for any Castform and switch it with Castform Sunny Form. (Any cards attached to Castform Sunny Form, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon.) Shuffle Castform Sunny Form back into your deck. You can't use more than 1 Temperament Poké-Power each turn.

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy attached to Castform Sunny Form.

flavorText: Its appearance changes with the weather. Recently, its molecules were found to be just like water.

name: Manaphy

attacks.name: Deep Sea Swirl
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

attacks.name: Life Saver
attacks.text: Put 2 Water Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Scyther.

flavorText: It moves so fast as to be invisible to the naked eye. Even when standing still, its camouflage prevents it from being seen.

name: Kadabra

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard 1 Psychic Energy card attached to Kadabra or this attack does nothing. Remove all damage counters from Kadabra.

flavorText: It emits special alpha waves from its body that induce headaches even to those just nearby.

name: Tauros

attacks.name: Stomp
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage; if tails, this attack does 20 damage.

attacks.name: Rampage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Tauros. Flip a coin. If tails, Tauros is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: When it targets an enemy, it charges furiously while whipping its body with its long tails.

name: Gallade-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Assault Sword
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has no Energy attached to it, this attack does 40 more damage.

attacks.name: Cross Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Cross Slash during your next turn.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Hand Trick
attacks.text: Return Aipom and all cards attached to it to your hand. (If you don't have any Benched Pokémon, this attack does nothing.)

flavorText: It lives atop giant trees. It wraps its tail around a branch so it won't fall off while asleep.

name: Crabominable

attacks.name: Gutsy Hammer
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself for each damage counter on it.

attacks.name: Double Stomp
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It aimed for the top but got lost and ended up on a snowy mountain. Being forced to endure the cold, this Pokémon evolved and grew fur.

name: Yanmega

attacks.name: Shoot Through
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: U-turn
attacks.text: Switch Yanmega with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: The beat of its wings is so powerful that it accidentally dislodges full-grown trees when it takes off in flight.

abilities.name: Major Tsunami
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Feraligatr is your Active Pokémon and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, your opponent switches his or her Active Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon. Either way, if you have any Benched Pokémon, switch Feraligatr with 1 of them. This power can't be used if Feraligatr is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Rending Jaws
attacks.text: If here are no damage counters on the Defending Pokémon, this attack's base damage is 40 instead of 70.

name: Hakamo-o

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It sheds and regrows its scales on a continuous basis. The scales become harder and sharper each time they're regrown.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Dark Guidance
attacks.text: Put a Basic Pokémon from your discard pile onto your Bench.

flavorText: It doggedly pursues its prey wherever it goes. However, the chase is abandoned at sunrise.

name: Espeon

abilities.name: Solar Revelation
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to each of your Pokémon that has any Energy attached to it.

attacks.name: Psy Report
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand.

flavorText: Its fur is so sensitive, it can sense minute shifts in the air and predict the weather.

name: Dragonite

ancientTrait.name: Δ Plus
ancientTrait.text: If your opponent's Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack of this Pokémon, take 1 more Prize card.

attacks.name: Wrapped in Wind
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to make its home somewhere in the sea. It guides crews of shipwrecks to shore.

name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Crane Neck
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its tail also has a small brain. It bites to repel any foe trying to sneak up on it from behind.

name: Unown [D]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Darkness]
abilities.text: Whenever a Darkness Pokémon damages 1 of your Pokémon, reduce that damage by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). This power stops working if you have more than 1 Unown [D] in play. (This power works even if Unown [D] is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.)

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

attacks.name: Mud Splash
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Aggron

attacks.name: Knock Back
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aura of the Land
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's). (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It claims an entire mountain as its own. The more wounds it has, the more it has battled, so don't take it lightly.

name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Aqua Shower
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Brine
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has any damage counters on it. This attack does 40 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It lives alone, away from others. Apparently, every one of them believes it is the most important.

name: Tympole

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: By vibrating its cheeks, it emits sound waves imperceptible to humans and warns others of danger.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Mismagius

attacks.name: Absorb Life
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its cry sounds like an incantation. It is said the cry may rarely be imbued with happiness-giving power.

attacks.name: Sniff Out
attacks.text: Put any 1 card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Powder Snow
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Corrosive Acid
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Natu

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.text: Switch Natu with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It usually forages for food on the ground but may, on rare occasions, hop onto branches to peck at shoots.

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: It hides any shiny object it finds in a secret location. Murkrow and Meowth loot one another's stashes.

attacks.name: Dragon Song
attacks.text: Each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Skorupi

attacks.name: Hone Claws
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Pierce attack's base damage is 90.

flavorText: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

name: Gothitelle

attacks.name: Mental Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: They can predict the future from the placement and movement of the stars. They can see Trainers' life spans.

name: Sobble
flavorText: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Razor Wind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Venipede
flavorText: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

name: Wimpod

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack doesn't happen.

flavorText: It will pick up anything it finds on the ground. Sometimes it finds coins, so Murkrow and Meowth will go after it.

name: Inteleon VMAX
rules: VMAX rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hydro Snipe
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

attacks.name: Max Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Inkay

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It flashes the light-emitting spots on its body, which drains its opponent's will to fight. It takes the opportunity to scuttle away and hide.

abilities.name: Rough Skin
abilities.text: If Carvanha is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Carvanha is Knocked Out), put 1 damage counter on the Attacking Pokémon.

abilities.name: Ancient Tentacles
abilities.text: Damage done to your opponent's Pokémon by your Omanyte, Omastar, Kabuto, Kabutops, or Kabutops ex isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Whimsicott

attacks.name: Windy Mischief
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Like the wind, it can slip through any gap, no matter how small. It leaves balls of white fluff behind.

name: Skiploom

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The bloom on top of its head opens and closes as the temperature fluctuates up and down.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Poison Up
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is Poisoned, put 3 more damage counters on that Pokémon between turns. This effect can be applied more than once.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Sharpshooting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: It has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.

name: Alolan Vulpix

abilities.name: Secret Alleyway
abilities.text: If you have any Fairy Pokémon in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: In hot weather, this Pokémon makes ice shards with its six tails and sprays them around to cool itself off.

name: Vikavolt

abilities.name: Strong Charge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may search your deck for a Grass Energy card and a Lightning Energy card and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Electro Cannon
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It produces electricity via an electrical organ in its abdomen. It overwhelms bird Pokémon with shocking beams of electrical energy.

name: Parasect

attacks.name: Spore
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: A host-parasite pair in which the parasite mushroom has taken over the host bug. Prefers damp places.

attacks.name: Pebble Hurl
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Arcanine

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its proud and regal appearance has captured the hearts of people since long ago.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Damage from this attack is reduced by 10 for each damage counter on Primeape.

attacks.name: Sudden Charge
attacks.text: Primeape does 10 damage to itself. Then, your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Cofagrigus

abilities.name: Six Feet Under
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack) you may Knock Out this Pokémon. If you do, put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Slap of Misfortune
attacks.text: Whenever your opponent flips a coin during his or her next turn, treat it as tails.

flavorText: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.

name: Dark Raticate

attacks.name: Hyper Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Raticate's front teeth are incredibly powerful; it can destroy a two-story house with them in 5 minutes.

name: Beartic

attacks.name: Frozen Slice
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It swims through frigid seas, searching for prey. From its frozen breath, it forms icy fangs that are harder than steel.

name: Machop
flavorText: It hefts a Graveler repeatedly to strengthen its entire body. It uses every type of martial arts.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

name: Monferno

attacks.name: Fire Tail Slap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Monferno.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It skillfully control the intensity of the fire on its tail to keep its foes at an ideal distance.

abilities.name: Energy Evolution
abilities.text: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to Eevee, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from Eevee that is the same type as the Energy card you attached to Eevee. Put that card onto Eevee. (This counts as evolving Eevee.) Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used when you attach an Energy card to Eevee as part of an attack's effect.

name: Pumpkaboo

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

abilities.name: Pure Body
abilities.text: To attach a Water Energy card from your hand to Suicune, you must discard an Energy card attached to Suicune. (Attach the Water Energy, and then discard an Energy card from Suicune.)

attacks.name: Hypno Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. If tails, this attack does 30 damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Golisopod

abilities.name: Emergency Exit
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has 2 or fewer Energy attached to it, it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: First Impression
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: The shell covering its body is as hard as diamond. This Pokémon will do anything it takes to win.

attacks.name: The Hula-la
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, both the Defending Pokémon and Spinda are now Confused.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Spinda.

name: Nidoking

attacks.name: Drag Off
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: King's Drum
attacks.text: If Nidoqueen is on your Bench, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: It is recognized by its rock-hard hide and its extended horn. Be careful with the horn, as it contains venom.

name: Unown [F]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Find]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown [F], Unown [I], Unown [N], and Unown [D] on your Bench, you may search your deck for a Trainer card. Show that card to your opponent, then put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Makuhita

attacks.name: Slap Down
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It toughens up by slamming into thick trees over and over. It gains a sturdy body and dauntless spirit.

name: Manectric

abilities.name: High Speed
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may draw 3 cards.

flavorText: It stimulates its own muscles with electricity, so it can move quickly. It eases its soreness with electricity, too, so it can recover quickly as well.

name: Togepi

attacks.name: Charm
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon attacks during your opponent's next turn, any damage it does is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Spike Ball Tackle
attacks.text: Togepi does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its shell is said to contain happiness itself, which it will share with those who are kind to it.

name: Sunflora

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Sunflora is now Confused (after doing damage).

flavorText: It converts sunlight into energy. In the darkness after sunset, it closes its petals and becomes still.

name: Poké Ball
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Charizard-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Crimson Storm
attacks.text: Discard 3 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Raging Out-GX
attacks.text: Discard the top 10 cards of your opponent's deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Zygarde-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Land's Pulse
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Cell Storm
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

name: Pinsir

attacks.name: Grip and Squeeze
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Power Guillotine
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is tails, this attack's base damage is 10 instead of 100.

flavorText: It grips prey with its pincers until the prey is torn in half. What it can't tear, it tosses far.

name: Blitzle

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

name: Rapidash

attacks.name: Overrun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Blaze Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy attached to Rapidash and this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It gallops at nearly 150 mph. With its mane blazing ferociously, it races as if it were an arrow.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.text: Drowzee can't be Asleep.

attacks.name: Soothing Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Pidgey

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)

flavorText: A common sight in forests and woods. It flaps its wings at ground level to kick up blinding sand.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Imitate
attacks.text: Draw cards until you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent.

flavorText: Its tail is so powerful that it can use it to grab a tree branch and hold itself up in the air.

attacks.name: Life Drain
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put a number of damage counters on the Defending Pokémon so that its remaining HP are 10.

name: Aipom

attacks.name: Last Resort
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It uses its tail to pluck fruits that are out of reach. Its tail is more adept than its real hands.

name: Crobat

abilities.name: Night Sight
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may draw a card.

attacks.name: Ultra-Toxic Fang
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on this Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: Having four wings allows it to fly more quickly and quietly so it can sneak up on prey without its noticing.

name: Drapion

attacks.name: Dangerous Stinger
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

abilities.name: Shining Horn
abilities.text: As long as Skarmory is the only Pokémon you have in play, your opponent's Basic Pokémon can't attack.

attacks.name: Cry for Help
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Metal Pokémon (excluding Pokémon-ex), show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Skarmory by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Gulpin

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: There is nothing its stomach can't digest. While it is digesting, vile, overpowering gases are expelled.

attacks.name: Aqua Trance
attacks.text: At the end of your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Whirlipede

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

name: Darmanitan

attacks.name: Synchrodraw
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: DarMAXitan
attacks.text: Flip a coin for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: When weakened in battle, it transforms into a stone statue. Then it sharpens its mind and fights on mentally.

name: Porygon2

abilities.name: Download
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Supporter card from your hand and use the effect of that card as the effect of this power. This power can't be used if Porygon2 is affected by a Special Condition.

flavorText: With planetary development software installed, it became capable of working in space.

name: Roggenrola

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to this Pokémon if that damage is 40 or less.

flavorText: Its ear is hexagonal in shape. Compressed underground, its body is as hard as steel.

name: Krabby
flavorText: If it senses danger approaching, it cloaks itself with bubbles from its mouth so it will look bigger.

attacks.name: Mend
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Metal Energy card and attach it to Steelix GL. If you do, remove 1 damage counter from Steelix GL.

attacks.name: Squeeze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 50 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Thunder Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its skin is very hard, so it is unhurt even if stepped on by sumo wrestlers. It smiles when transmitting electricity.

name: Kakuna

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Kakuna during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself from predators.

name: Toxicroak

abilities.name: Poison Enzyme
abilities.text: Prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from your opponent's Poisoned Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

name: Kommo-o

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Raging Uppercut
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has 8 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 120 more damage.

flavorText: Its rigid scales function as offense and defense. In the past, its scales were processed and used to make weapons and other commodities.

name: Burmy Trash Cloak

abilities.name: Cloak Evolution
abilities.text: Burmy Trash Cloak can evolve during the turn you play it.

flavorText: Even if it is born where there are no cocooning materials, it somehow always ends up with a cloak.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Slack Off
attacks.text: Remove all damage counters from Slakoth. Slakoth can't attack during your next turn.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Quagsire but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Steady Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Misty's Starmie but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Centiskorch

attacks.name: Hundred Foot Flames
attacks.text: For each Fire Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.

abilities.name: Fire Immunity
abilities.text: You can't attach Fire Energy cards from your hand to Moltres.

attacks.name: Collect Fire
attacks.text: If there are any Fire Energy cards in your discard pile, flip a coin. If heads, attach 1 of them to Moltres.

attacks.name: Burning Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Moltres.

name: Kyurem

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Glaciate
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It generates powerful, freezing energy inside itself, but its body became frozen when the energy leaked out.

name: Floette

abilities.name: Flower Veil
abilities.text: Each of your Grass Pokémon in play gets +20 HP.

flavorText: It flutters around fields of flowers and cares for flowers that are starting to wilt. It draws out the hidden power of flowers to battle.

name: Golbat

attacks.name: Pulse Search
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand.

flavorText: It loves the blood of humans and Pokémon. It flies around at night in search of neck veins.
