attacks.name: Thunder Wave
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.text: Flaaffy does 10 damage to itself.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Fighting Fury
abilities.text: Each of your Fighting Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Machamp Crush
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its four ruggedly developed arms can launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds.

name: Shuckle

abilities.name: Hard Shell
abilities.text: Whenever an attack (including your own) does 40 or less damage to Shuckle (after applying Weakness and Resistance), reduce that damage to 10. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.) This power stops working while Shuckle is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Toxic Saliva
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. It now takes 20 Poison damage instead of 10 after each player's turn (even if it was already Poisoned).

flavorText: It stores berries inside its shell. To avoid attacks, it hides beneath rocks and remains completely still.

name: Garbodor

abilities.name: Garbotoxin
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, each Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities (except for Garbotoxin).

flavorText: It clenches opponents with its left arm and finishes them off with foul-smelling poison gas belched from its mouth.

name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Hitmonchan during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its punches slice the air. However, it seems to need a short break after fighting for three minutes.

name: Onix

abilities.name: Energy Healer
abilities.text: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to Onix, remove a damage counter from Onix.

attacks.name: Boundless Power
attacks.text: Onix can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: It rapidly bores through the ground at 50 mph by squirming and twisting its massive, rugged body.

name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

name: Bibarel

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: A river dammed by Bibarel will never overflow its banks, which is appreciated by people nearby.

name: Duosion
flavorText: Since they have two divided brains, at times they suddenly try to take two different actions at once.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Call for Friends
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Wobbuffet.

abilities.name: Assistance
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Wigglytuff is on your Bench, you may choose 1 of your Active Pokémon and remove 1 Special Condition from it.

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Wigglytuff by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Defense Curl
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Discharge
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: The long hairs on its back act as lightning rods. The bolts of lightning it attracts are stored as energy in its electric sac.

name: Giratina-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Renegade Pulse
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Mega Evolution Pokémon.

attacks.name: Chaos Wheel
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon Tool, Special Energy, or Stadium cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

name: Slowking

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Hand Press
attacks.text: If you have more cards in your hand than your opponent, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Being bitten by Shellder gave it intelligence comparable to that of award-winning scientists.

name: Mewtwo

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Barrier
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon. If 1 of your Pokémon used Barrier during your last turn, this attack can't be used.

flavorText: A Pokémon whose genetic code was repeatedly recombined for research. It turned vicious as a result.

abilities.name: Overeager
abilities.text: If Sableye is your Active Pokémon at the beginning of the game, you go first. (If each player's Active Pokémon has the Overeager Poké-Body, this power does nothing.)

attacks.name: Impersonate
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card and discard it. Shuffle your deck afterward. Then, use the effect of that card as the effect of this attack.

attacks.name: Overconfident
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Sableye, this attack's base damage is 40.

name: Malamar

abilities.name: Psychic Recharge
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lures prey close with hypnotic motions, then wraps its tentacles around it before finishing it off with digestive fluids.

name: Ducklett

attacks.name: Aerial Ace
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: When attacked, it uses its feathers to splash water, escaping under cover of the spray.

name: Jirachi-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Stellar Guidance
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Hypnostrike
attacks.text: Both this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.

attacks.name: Flip Over
attacks.text: Beldum does 10 damage to itself, and don't apply Weakness and Resistance to this damage.

name: Erika's Hospitality
rules: You can play this card only if you have 4 or fewer other cards in your hand.
Draw a card for each of your opponent's Pokémon in play.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Cramorant

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pump
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.

name: Drizzile

abilities.name: Shady Dealings
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for a Trainer card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

name: Magnemite

abilities.name: Sparkling Induction
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, its Retreat Cost is Colorless less for each Magnemite on your Bench.

flavorText: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.

name: Diglett

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)

flavorText: It follows Onixes in their tunnels, looking for scraps of food they have left behind.

name: Altaria

ancientTrait.name: Δ Evolution
ancientTrait.text: You may play this card from your hand to evolve a Pokémon during your first turn or the turn you play that Pokémon.

abilities.name: Clear Humming
abilities.text: Each of your Colorless Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

name: Salazzle

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Severe Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit that it lives with.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Pelipper but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Sleep Inducer
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. The new Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It remembers every dream it eats. It rarely eats the dreams of adults because children's are much tastier.

name: Chinchou
flavorText: It shoots positive and negative electricity between the tips of its two antennae and zaps its enemies.

abilities.name: Chaos Move
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if your opponent has 3 or fewer Prizes, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 Pokémon (yours or your opponent's) to another (even if it would Knock Out the other Pokémon). This power can't be used if Gengar is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Hide in Shadows
attacks.text: Switch Gengar with 1 of your Benched Pokémon, if any.

name: Wishiwashi

attacks.name: Deep Sea Swirl
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 8 cards.

flavorText: Individually, they're incredibly weak. It's by gathering up into schools that they're able to confront opponents.

name: Machoke
flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful, it must wear a power-save belt to be able to regulate its motions.

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Night Roam
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on each Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Anyone who dares peer into its body to see its spectral ball of fire will have their spirit stolen away.

name: Altaria

attacks.name: Song of Echoes
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Midnight Eyes
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: On sunny days, it flies freely through the sky and blends into the clouds. It sings in a beautiful soprano.

name: Weepinbell

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: It spits out poisonpowder to immobilize the enemy, and then finishes the enemy with a spray of acid.

name: Dragonair

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: If its body takes on an aura, the weather changes instantly. It is said to live in seas and lakes.

name: Wobbuffet
flavorText: It desperately tries to keep its black tail hidden. It is said to be proof the tail hides a secret.

name: Skiploom

attacks.name: Mysterious Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It spreads its petals to absorb sunlight. It also floats in the air to get closer to the sun.

attacks.name: Magic Darts
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon and flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads to that Pokémon. Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Carvanha.

name: Mawile

attacks.name: Selfish Draw
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your deck. You may put it into your hand. If not, discard it and draw a card.

attacks.name: Destructive Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It chomps with its gaping mouth. Its huge jaws are actually steel horns that have been transformed.

name: Unown [M]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Metal]
abilities.text: Whenever a Metal Pokémon damages 1 of your Pokémon, reduce that damage by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). This power stops working if you have more than 1 Unown [M] in play. (This power works even if Unown [M] is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.)

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Honchkrow

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Nightmare Mambo
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Asleep, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: If one utters a deep cry, many Murkrow gather quickly. For this, it is called "Summoner of Night."

name: Magnemite

attacks.name: Shock Generator
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It moves while constantly hovering. It discharges Thunder Wave and so on from the units at its sides.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Pounce
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Persian during your next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: Although its fur has many admirers, it is tough to raise as a pet because of its fickle meanness.

abilities.name: Evolution Helper
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Nidoqueen is on your Bench, you may search your deck for a card that evolves from your Active Pokémon and attach it to your Active Pokémon. (This counts as evolving that Pokémon.) Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Double Claw
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads

name: Drizzile
flavorText: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.

name: Purugly

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is a brazen brute that barges its way into another Pokémon's nest and claims it as its own.

name: Elgyem

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent's brain, causing unendurable headaches.

attacks.name: Burning Claw
attacks.text: You may discard a Fire Energy card attached to Quilava. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Dubwool V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Soft Wool
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Revenge Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

name: Zubat

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

flavorText: It sleeps in caves during the day. It has no eyes, so to check its surroundings while flying, it emits ultrasonic waves.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

name: Cofagrigus

attacks.name: Elongating Arms
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Crazy Slap
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Grave robbers who mistake them for real coffins and get too close end up trapped inside their bodies.

abilities.name: Rock Body
abilities.text: All damage done by attacks to Golem is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: Don't apply Resistance.

name: Gourgeist

attacks.name: Eerie Voice
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spirit Scream
attacks.text: Put damage counters on both Active Pokémon until the remaining HP of each Pokémon is 10.

flavorText: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Rock Cannon
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rock Slide
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to 2 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: GRAVELER make their homes on sheer cliff faces by gouging out numerous horizontal holes.

abilities.name: Sludge Cell
abilities.text: If Muk remains affected by any Special Conditions between turns, remove 2 damage counters from Muk.

attacks.name: Strange Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, Muk is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Strange Sludge
attacks.text: If Muk is Poisoned, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Bellsprout and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench is full.)

flavorText: A carnivorous Pokémon that traps and eats bugs. It uses its root feet to soak up needed moisture.

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rainbow Star
attacks.text: Does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

attacks.name: Net Force
attacks.text: Does 40 damage times the number of Reuniclus you have in play.

flavorText: When Reuniclus shake hands, a network forms between their brains, increasing their psychic power.

name: Arceus LV.X
rules: You may have as many of this card in your deck as you like.
Put this card onto your Active Arceus. Arceus LV.X can use any attack, Poké-Power, or Poké-Body from its previous level.

abilities.name: Multitype
abilities.text: Arceus LV.X's type is the same as its previous Level.

attacks.name: Meteor Blast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack's base damage is 50 instead of 100.

name: Scyther

attacks.name: Swords Dance
attacks.text: During your next turn, Scyther's Slashing Strike attack's base damage is 60.

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, Scyther can't use Slashing Strike.

flavorText: It is nearly impossible to parry its attacking scythes. Its movements are like a ninja's.

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Dive
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: It blinds prey with an intense burst of light. With the prey incapacitated, the Pokémon swallows it in a single gulp.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Rapidash.

attacks.name: Gallop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard a Fire Energy card attached to Rapidash and this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Positive Ion
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

attacks.name: Negative Ion
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Rising Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Gothita

attacks.name: Future Sight
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck and put them back on top of your deck in any order.

flavorText: They intently observe both Trainers and Pokémon. Apparently, they are looking at something that only Gothita can see.

name: Zygarde

attacks.name: Core Enforcer
attacks.text: Discard a Darkness Energy and a Fairy Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This is Zygarde's form at times when it uses its overwhelming power to suppress those who endanger the ecosystem.

abilities.name: Free Flight
abilities.text: If Gligar has no Energy cards attached to it, Gligar's Retreat Cost is 0.

attacks.name: Toxic Grip
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Bagon

attacks.name: Continuous Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

name: Machoke

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 50 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on Machoke.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Machoke does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Its muscular body is so powerful that it must wear a power-save belt to help regulate its motions.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Continuous Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Using telepathy only they can employ, they always form a cluster of six Exeggcute.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

name: Heliolisk

abilities.name: Dry Skin
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by an attack from 1 of your opponent's Water Pokémon is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

name: Kingler

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its large and hard pincer has 10,000-horsepower strength. However, being so big, it is unwieldy to move.

attacks.name: Drawup Power
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Breloom

attacks.name: Hover Hit
attacks.text: The Retreat Cost for the Defending Pokémon is 0 until the end of your next turn.

attacks.name: Crash Bomber
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Special Energy cards attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 40 more damage. Then, discard a Special Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

attacks.name: Poison Spurt
attacks.text: Discard a Grass Energy card attached to Grimer. The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Crystal Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't attach Energy cards to the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Suction from its nostrils enables it to stick to cave walls during sleep. It leaves a heart-shaped mark behind.

name: Unown [C]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Chase]
abilities.text: As long as Unown [C] is your Active Pokémon, whenever your opponent's Active Pokémon tries to retreat, flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on that Pokémon. Apply Weakness and Resistance.

flavorText: It is believed that the variety of types of this Pokémon were created by evolutionary adaptation, as each possesses a different ability.

name: Arrokuda
flavorText: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It's very proud of that jaw.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Pikachu does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: When several of these Pokémon gather, their electricity can cause lightning storms.

name: Sneasel

attacks.name: Nyan Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

name: Escavalier

attacks.name: Steamroll
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Slashing Strike
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Slashing Strike during your next turn.

flavorText: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

name: Spiritomb

abilities.name: Cursed Whirlpool
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, your opponent's Active Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Cursed Drop
attacks.text: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

name: Giovanni's Exile
rules: Discard up to 2 of your Benched Pokémon that have no damage counters on them and all cards attached to them.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Scolipede

attacks.name: Steamroller
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Poison Claws
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Highly aggressive, it uses the claws near its neck to dig into its opponent and poison them.

name: Mareanie

attacks.name: Regeneration
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Poison Tentacles
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The first symptom of its sting is numbness. The next is an itching sensation so intense that it's impossible to resist the urge to claw at your skin.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Tail Strike
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Skrelp
flavorText: It looks just like rotten kelp. It hides from foes while storing up power for its evolution.

name: Drifblim

attacks.name: Expand
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Drifblim by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Whirlwind
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It's drowzy in daytime, but flies off in the evening in big groups. No one knows where they go.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: X Ball
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psydrive
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Fly
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Tropius during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Blessed Fruit
attacks.text: Remove all damage counters from 1 of your Benched Grass Pokémon.

abilities.name: Extra Protection
abilities.text: As long as you have Illumise in play, prevent all effects, including damage, done to Volbeat by attacks from your opponent's Pokémon-ex.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Zygarde-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Land's Pulse
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Cell Storm
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Oranguru

attacks.name: Fixer of the Forest
attacks.text: Put 3 Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.

flavorText: Known for its extreme intelligence, this Pokémon will look down on inexperienced Trainers, so it's best suited to veteran Trainers.

name: Floette

attacks.name: Petal Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It flutters around fields of flowers and cares for flowers that are starting to wilt. It draws out the hidden power of flowers to battle.

name: Machamp

abilities.name: Fighting Fury
abilities.text: Each of your Fighting Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Machamp Crush
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done by attacks from the Defending Pokémon is reduced by 40 (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its four ruggedly developed arms can launch a flurry of 1,000 punches in just two seconds.

name: Dusknoir

abilities.name: Dark Invitation
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may have your opponent reveal their hand. Put a Basic Pokémon you find there onto your opponent's Bench, and put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Jack
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It is said to take lost spirits into its pliant body and guide them home.

name: Ho-Oh-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Sacred Fire
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Phoenix Burn
attacks.text: This Pokémon can't use Phoenix Burn during your next turn.

attacks.name: Eternal Light-GX
attacks.text: Put 3 in any combination of Fire Pokémon-GX or Fire Pokémon-EX from your discard pile onto your Bench. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Drive Off
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Swellow is your Active Pokémon, you may switch 1 of the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Your opponent picks the Benched Pokémon to switch. This power can't be used if Swellow is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Tail Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: X-ray photos were taken to check its body structure. Nothing appeared, however.

name: Blitzle
flavorText: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

name: Energy Search
rules: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Pokémon Center Lady
rules: Heal 60 damage from 1 of your Pokémon, and it recovers from all Special Conditions.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Spoink

attacks.name: Flail Around
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Using its tail like a spring, it keeps its heart beating by bouncing constantly. If it stops, it dies.

name: Geodude

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Found in fields and mountains. Mistaking them for boulders, people often step or trip on them.

attacks.name: Vital Pinchers
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Goomy

abilities.name: Water Down
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, you may search your deck for Goomy and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It's covered in a slimy membrane that makes any punches or kicks slide off it harmlessly.

name: Pyukumuku

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: The tradition known as Pyukumuku chucking started from the custom of throwing Pyukumuku back into the sea after they wash onshore.

name: Kangaskhan

attacks.name: Fetch
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: One-Two Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The child in its pouch leaves home after roughly three years. That is the only time the mother is heard to cry wildly.

name: Zebstrika

abilities.name: Zap Zone
abilities.text: Damage from the attacks of your Lightning Pokémon isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crashing Bolt
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

name: Mewtwo-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Energy Absorption
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Regeneration
attacks.text: Heal 60 damage from this Pokémon.

name: Quagsire

abilities.name: Aqua Healing
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Quagsire is your Active Pokémon and the Defending Pokémon has any Water Energy attached to it, you may remove 3 damage counters from Quagsire.

attacks.name: Muddy Water
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has a sluggish nature. It lies at the river's bottom, waiting for prey to stray into its mouth.

name: Magikarp

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Magikarp.

flavorText: In the distant past, it was stronger than its horribly weak descendants that exist today.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Its cheeks hold poison sacs. It tries to catch foes off guard to jab them with toxic fingers.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Taunt
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and switch it with 1 of the Defending Pokémon. Your opponent chooses the Defending Pokémon to switch.

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Octillery

attacks.name: Water Vein
attacks.text: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. Flip a coin for each Energy card you find there. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads. Shuffle the revealed cards back into your deck.

attacks.name: Octazooka
attacks.text: Any time the Defending Pokémon tries to attack, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing. (If the Defending Pokémon is no longer your opponent's Active Pokémon, this effect ends.)

flavorText: It loves to lurk inside holes in rocks. It sometimes sprays ink on prey by sticking out only its mouth.

name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Poliwag but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. Extra Water Energy after the 2nd don't count.

flavorText: Its newly grown legs prevent it from running. It appears to prefer swimming over trying to stand.

name: Toxapex

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.

attacks.name: Poisonous Whip
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: To survive in the cold waters of Galar, this Pokémon forms a dome with its legs, enclosing its body so it can capture its own body heat.

name: Weavile

abilities.name: Tear Away
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may put a Pokémon Tool card attached to 1 of your Pokémon into your hand.

flavorText: They live in cold regions, forming groups of four or five that hunt prey with impressive coordination.

name: Shelgon

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

name: Charmander

attacks.name: Call for Friends
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Basic Pokémon, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The fire on the tip of its tail is a measure of its life. If healthy, its tail burns intensely.

name: Hitmonlee
flavorText: If it starts kicking repeatedly, both legs will stretch even longer to strike a fleeing foe.

name: Aron

attacks.name: Mountain Eater
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. Then, remove 2 damage counters from Aron.

flavorText: When it evolves, it sheds the steel carapace that covered its whole body and develops a new one.

name: Roggenrola

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: They were discovered a hundred years ago in an earthquake fissure. Inside each one is an energy core.

name: Togepi

attacks.name: Poison Barb
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: A proverb claims that happiness will come to anyone who can make a sleeping Togepi stand up.

name: Gothitelle

attacks.name: Tractorbeam
attacks.text: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to the new Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Link Blast
attacks.text: If this Pokémon and your opponent's Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached to them, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

name: Slowpoke

attacks.name: Yawn
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Alolan home cooking involves drying Slowpoke tails and then simmering them into a salty stew.

name: Swanna

attacks.name: Tailwind
attacks.text: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Air Slash
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Despite their elegant appearance, they can flap their wings strongly and fly for thousands of miles.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Tentacool

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It drifts through the sea searching for prey. Its poisonous tentacles break off sometimes, but after a while, they grow back.

name: Dratini

attacks.name: Wrap
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: The capture of the first Dratini inspired the search for other legendary Pokémon.

name: Drowzee

attacks.name: Focused Wish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: If you think that you had a good dream but you can't remember it, a Drowzee has probably eaten it.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: It twists and squirms through the ground. The thunderous roar of its tunneling echoes a long way.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Storm Up
attacks.text: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage. Discard that Stadium card.

attacks.name: Crash Impact
attacks.text: Rhydon does 10 damage to itself. Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon, if any.

flavorText: Its brain developed after it stood up on its hind legs. Its drill horn bores tunnels through solid rock.

name: Exeggutor

attacks.name: Psychic Strategy
attacks.text: Each player counts the number of cards in his or her opponent's hand. Each player shuffles his or her hand into his or her deck. Then, each player draws a number of cards up to the number of cards his or her opponent had. (You draw your cards first.)

attacks.name: Super Eggsplosion
attacks.text: Discard as many Energy cards as you like attached to your Pokémon. For each Energy card you discarded, flip a coin. This attack does 50 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It is called "The Walking Jungle." If a head grows too big, it falls off and becomes an Exeggcute.

name: Sliggoo

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Tail Rap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its cute act is a ruse. When victims let down their guard, they find their items taken. It attacks with sharp claws.

name: Meowstic

abilities.name: Mysterious Ears
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Psyblast
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust.

name: Jessie & James
rules: Each player discards 2 cards from their hand. Your opponent discards first.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Swalot

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.text: If, before doing damage, your opponent's Active Pokémon has less remaining HP than this Pokémon, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It gulps anything that fits in its mouth. Its special enzymes can dissolve anything.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

name: Girafarig

attacks.name: Show Off
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Its tail has a small brain of its own. Beware! If you get close, it may react to your scent and bite.

name: Zebstrika
flavorText: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

name: Nidoking

attacks.name: Lovestrike
attacks.text: Does 40 more damage for each Nidoqueen on your Bench.

flavorText: One swing of its mighty tail can snap a telephone pole as if it were a matchstick.

name: Carnivine

attacks.name: Swallow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove from Carnivine the number of damage counters equal to the damage you did to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Graveler

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: With a free and uncaring nature, it doesn't mind if pieces break off while it rolls down mountains.

attacks.name: Dig Under
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

name: Bagon

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Almost Flight
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

attacks.name: Sleep Poison
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

abilities.name: Aqua Lift
abilities.text: If Lotad has any Water Energy attached to it, the Retreat Cost for Lotad is 0.

name: Bagon
flavorText: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

name: Happiny

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chansey from your hand onto Happiny (this counts as evolving Happiny) and remove all damage counters from Happiny.

attacks.name: Lively
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon.

flavorText: It loves round white things. It carries and egg-shaped rock in imitation of CHANSEY.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Giant Claw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Poochyena

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: A Pokémon with a persistent nature, it chases its chosen prey until the prey becomes exhausted.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Vigoroth.

name: Charizard V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fire Spin
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

name: Relicanth

attacks.name: Primal Calling
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Trainer Cards that have Fossil in their names, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Sleep Swirl
attacks.text: Both Relicanth and the Defending Pokémon are now Asleep.

flavorText: A rare Pokémon discovered during a deep-sea exploration. It has not changed in over 100 million years.

name: Diglett

attacks.name: Mine
attacks.text: Look at the top card of your opponent's deck. Then, you may have your opponent shuffle his or her deck.

flavorText: Lives about one yard underground where it feeds on plant roots. It sometimes appears aboveground.

name: Umbreon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Veil of Darkness
attacks.text: Discard as many cards as you like from your hand. Then, draw that many cards.

attacks.name: Endgame
attacks.text: If your opponent's Mega Evolution Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards.

name: Goldeen

attacks.name: Elegant Swim
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Although known for their splendid tail fins, Goldeen apparently compete among themselves to see whose horn is thickest and sharpest.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Swallow Up
attacks.text: Before doing damage, count the remaining HP of the Defending Pokémon and Swalot. If the Defending Pokémon has fewer remaining HP than Swalot's, this attack does 50 damage plus 30 more damage.

name: Reuniclus

attacks.name: Dizzy Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: These remarkably intelligent Pokémon fight by controlling arms that can grip with rock-crushing power.

name: Gambler
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Flip a coin. If heads, draw 8 cards. If tails, draw 1 card.

attacks.name: Fire Tail Slap
attacks.text: Use this attack only if there are any Fire Energy cards attached to Blaine's Charmander. Flip a coin. If tails, discard 1 of those cards.

name: Potion
rules: Heal 30 damage from 1 of your Pokémon.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

name: Yamask

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a card at random from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

flavorText: Each of them carries a mask that used to be its face when it was human. Sometimes they look at it and cry.

name: Solosis

attacks.name: Cell Culture
attacks.text: Search your deck for Solosis and put it into your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: Because their bodies are enveloped in a special liquid, they can survive in any environment.

name: Golbat

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Acrobatics
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its thick fangs are hollow like straws, making them unexpectedly fragile. These fangs are specialized for sucking blood.

name: Barraskewda

attacks.name: Spiral Jet
attacks.text: Discard 2 Water Energy cards from your hand. If you don't, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Pokémon has a jaw that's as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda's flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.

name: Buneary

attacks.name: Drawup Power
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Extend Ears
attacks.text: Remove 1 damage counter from each of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Dragalge

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Twister
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. For each heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon. If both of them are tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Tales are told of ships that wander into seas where Dragalge live, never to return.

name: Nihilego-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Empty Light
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Symbiont-GX
attacks.text: Add the top 2 cards of your opponent's deck to their Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

name: Mew

abilities.name: Neutral Shield
abilities.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent's Evolution Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: So rare that it is still said to be a mirage by many experts. Only a few people have seen it worldwide.

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Brain Control
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals his or her hand. Choose a card from there and put it on the bottom of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It can manipulate an opponent's memory. Apparently, it communicates by flashing its three different-colored fingers.

abilities.name: Primal Stare
abilities.text: As long as Omastar is your Active Pokémon, your opponent can't play Basic Pokémon or Evolution cards from his or her hand to evolve his or her Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Spook
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

abilities.name: Poison Resistance
abilities.text: Scizor can't be Poisoned.

attacks.name: Snatch
attacks.text: Before doing damage, you may choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with no damage counters on it and switch the Defending Pokémon with it.

attacks.name: Heavy Metal
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the amount of Metal Energy attached to Scizor. This attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Inteleon

abilities.name: Shady Dealings
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Aqua Bullet
attacks.text: This attack also does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

name: Reshiram-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Flame Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Fire Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Scorching Column
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Vermilion-GX
attacks.text: You may attach up to 5 Fire Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Exact Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Fighting Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, base damage of Vigoroth's Slash is attack is 90 instead of 40.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Tight Jaw
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

name: Vibrava

attacks.name: Energy Typhoon
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of Energy cards in your opponent's discard pile. Then, put those Energy cards on top of your opponent's deck. Your opponent shuffles his or her deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It violently shudders its wings, generating ultrasonic waves to induce headaches in people.

name: Bewear

attacks.name: Mix-Up
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Tantrum
attacks.text: This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

attacks.name: Night Spirits
attacks.text: Flip a number of coins equal to the total number of Sabrina's Gastlys, Sabrina's Haunters, and Sabrina's Gengars you have in play. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Toxic gas is held within its thin, balloon-shaped body, so it can cause massive explosions.

name: Gothorita

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Mind Shock
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

flavorText: Starlight is the source of their power. At night, they mark star positions by using psychic power to float stones.

name: Florges

attacks.name: Brilliant Search
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Petal Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It claims exquisite flower gardens as its territory, and it obtains power from basking in the energy emitted by flowering plants.

name: Unown [P]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Perform]
abilities.text: If an attack damaged Unown [P] during your opponent's last turn and Unown [P] was your Active Pokémon, Unown [P]'s Hidden Power attack does that much more damage to the Defending Pokémon. This power can be used even if Unown [P] is Confused.

flavorText: It is believed that the variety of types of this Pokémon were created by evolutionary adaptation, as each possesses a different ability.

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to Corphish by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Unown [N]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Normal]
abilities.text: Whenever a Colorless Pokémon damages 1 of your Pokémon, reduce that damage by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). This power stops working if you have more than 1 Unown [N] in play. (This power works even if Unown [N] is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.)

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Poliwhirl

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Capable of living in or out of water. When out of water, it sweats to keep its body slimy.

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Gnaw Off
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Sneaky Attack
attacks.text: If Raticate has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It whittles its constantly growing fangs by gnawing on hard things. It can chew apart cinder walls.

name: Magneton

attacks.name: Static Shock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Electro Ball
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Generates strange radio signals. It raises the temperature by 3.6 degrees Fahrenheit within 3,300 feet.

name: Seaking

attacks.name: Enhanced Horn
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool card attached to it, flip 6 coins instead.

flavorText: Its horn spins like a drill to steadily hollow out rocks—even harder ones. The coloration of the male is more vivid.

name: Shelgon

abilities.name: Exoskeleton
abilities.text: Any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 10 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Its armored body makes all attacks bounce off. The armor is too tough, however, making it heavy and somewhat sluggish.

name: Unown [Q]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Quicken]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to any of your Pokémon with Unown in its name during your opponent's next turn. If you have more than 1 Unown [Q] in play, use only 1 [Quicken] each turn. This power can be used even if Unown [Q] is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is believed that the variety of types of this Pokémon were created by evolutionary adaptation, as each possesses a different ability.

name: Ponyta
flavorText: Its hooves are 10 times harder than diamonds. It can trample anything flat in moments.

attacks.name: Sleep Pulse
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Regirock

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrier
ancientTrait.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Land Maker
attacks.text: Put 2 Stadium cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: The same rocks that form its body have been found in ground layers around the world.

name: Oricorio

abilities.name: Vital Dance
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 basic Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Oricorio relaxes by swaying gently. This increases its psychic energy, which it then fires at its enemies.

name: Raticate

attacks.name: Super Fang
attacks.text: Does damage to the Defending Pokémon equal to half the Defending Pokémon's remaining HP (rounded up to the nearest 10).

flavorText: It uses its whiskers to maintain its balance. It seems to slow down if they are cut off.

name: Koga's Trap
rules: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

attacks.name: Paralyzing Gaze
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

abilities.name: Terraforming
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 4 cards of your deck and rearrange them as you like. This power can't be used if Machamp is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Iron Fist
attacks.text: Count the number of Pokémon you have in play with damage counters on them. Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 10 more damage for each of those Pokémon.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It has the peculiar power of being able to see emotions such as joy and rage in the form of waves.

name: Mankey

attacks.name: Three-Step Strike
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: It can spontaneously become enraged. Everyone near it clears out as it rampages and the resulting loneliness makes it angrier still.

attacks.name: Fast Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for an Evolution card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Double Scratch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Spiritomb

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Damage Play
attacks.text: Move as many damage counters on your opponent's Benched Pokémon as you like to any of your opponent's other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

name: Black Kyurem

attacks.name: Frozen Wings
attacks.text: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dazzling Blizzard
attacks.text: If you have any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

name: Drowzee

abilities.name: Long-Distance Hypnosis
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep; if tails, your Active Pokémon is now Asleep. This power can't be used if Drowzee is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Nightmare
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Not popular with girls because of its attitude. Can often be heard muttering to itself in a low voice.

attacks.name: Double Spin
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

name: Inteleon

attacks.name: Silent Shot
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Hydro Snipe
attacks.text: You may put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.

name: Prinplup

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Because every PRINPLUP considers itself to be the most important, they can never form a group.

name: Goodra

attacks.name: Liquid Blow
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon for each Colorless in its Retreat Cost. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Shining Breath
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon can't be affected by any Special Conditions.

flavorText: It attacks with retractable horns. It throws a punch that's the equivalent of the force of a hundred pro boxers.

name: Professor's Research (Professor Magnolia)
rules: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

name: Boldore

attacks.name: Smack Down
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has Fighting Resistance, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: When it overflows with power, the orange crystal on its body glows. It looks for underground water in caves.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Pay Day
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Adores circular objects. Wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Unown [U]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Undo]
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if you have Unown [U], Unown [N], Unown [D], and Unown [O] on your Bench, you may return your Active Pokémon and all cards attached to it to your hand.

flavorText: Their shapes look like hieroglyphs on ancient tablets. It is said that the two are somehow related.

name: Poliwag

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: The direction of its belly spiral differs by area. The equator is thought to have an effect on this.

name: Weezing

abilities.name: Aftermath
abilities.text: When this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack, discard the top 3 cards of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Smogbank
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Inhaling toxic fumes from trash and mixing them inside its body lets it spread an even fouler stench.

name: Lapras

attacks.name: Aqua Wash
attacks.text: You may put 2 Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

flavorText: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

attacks.name: Good Night
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Good Morning
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon was Asleep, it is no longer Asleep.

attacks.name: Pure Power
attacks.text: Put 2 damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Mach Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Despite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speeds over 180 mph.

name: Magnezone

abilities.name: Magnetic Circuit
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may attach a Lightning Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.text: Discard a Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It evolved from exposure to a special magnetic field. Three units generate magnetism.

name: Dugtrio

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Rock Tumble
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

name: Slugma

attacks.name: Magma Ring
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It never sleeps. It has to keep moving because if it stopped, its magma body would cool and harden.

attacks.name: Gouge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage.

name: Recycle
rules: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card in your discard pile on top of your deck.

name: Growlithe
flavorText: It has a brave and trustworthy nature. It fearlessly stands up to bigger and stronger foes.

attacks.name: Rest
attacks.text: Remove all Special Conditions and 4 damage counters from Wailmer (all if there are less than 4). Wailmer is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Water Gun
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each Water Energy attached to Wailmer but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

abilities.name: Uplifting Glow
abilities.text: As long as Illumise is in play, Volbeat's Retreat Cost is 0.

attacks.name: Toxic Vibration
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Pester
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Crobat

attacks.name: Triple Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 3 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Surprise Strike
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Both its legs became wings, and as a result, it can't move well on the ground. All it can do is crawl around.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.text: Your opponent switches the Defending Pokémon with 1 of his or her Benched Pokémon.

name: Gothitelle

attacks.name: Doom Decree
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, the Defending Pokémon is Knocked Out.

attacks.name: Black Magic
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Starry skies thousands of light-years away are visible in the space distorted by their intense psychic power.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Electabuzz from your hand onto Elekid (this counts as evolving Elekid) and remove all damage counters from Elekid.

attacks.name: Thunder Spear
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 10 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Tapu Fini

attacks.name: Nature Wave
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Ultra Beasts in play, this attack can be used for Colorless.

flavorText: This guardian deity of Poni Island manipulates water. Because it lives deep within a thick fog, it came to be both feared and revered.

attacks.name: Doubleslap
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Freezing Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, the Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Trample
attacks.text: For each Benched Pokémon (yours and your opponent's), flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

abilities.name: Dense Body
abilities.text: Any damage done to Slowbro by attacks from Basic Pokémon (excluding Baby Pokémon) is reduced by 20.

attacks.name: Energy Cannon
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage plus 10 damage for each Energy attached to Slowbro but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

name: Seviper

attacks.name: Tail Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play any Trainer cards from his or her hand during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Poison Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: For many generations, it has feuded with ZANGOOSE. It whets its bladed tail on rocks for battle.

name: Cherubi
flavorText: The small ball is not only filled with nutrients, it is also tasty. Starly try to peck it off.

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Snivel
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Cubone during your opponent's next turn, any damage done by the attack is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Cubone.

flavorText: Because it never removes its skull helmet, no one has ever seen this Pokémon's real face.

name: Joltik
flavorText: Joltik can be found clinging to other Pokémon. It's soaking up static electricity because it can't produce a charge on its own.

name: Xatu

attacks.name: Prophecy
attacks.text: Look at the top 3 cards of either player's deck and rearrange them as you like.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: They say that it stays still and quiet because it is seeing both the past and future at the same time.

name: Mankey
flavorText: It lives in treetop colonies. If one becomes enraged, the whole colony rampages for no reason.

name: Spritzee

attacks.name: Fickle Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It emits a scent that enraptures those who smell it. This fragrance changes depending on what it has eaten.

name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Bullet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

name: Mightyena

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It will always obey the commands of a skilled Trainer. Its behavior arises from its living in packs in ancient times.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: The aura that emanates from its body intensifies to alert others if it is afraid or sad.

name: Duosion

attacks.name: Recover
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon and heal all damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When their brains, now divided in two, are thinking the same thought, these Pokémon exhibit their maximum power.

name: Shelgon

attacks.name: Knock Away
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

name: Elgyem
