attacks.name: Calm Mind
attacks.text: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon had never been seen until it appeared from far in the desert 50 years ago.

name: Hypno

abilities.name: Goodnight, Babies
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may leave both Active Pokémon Asleep.

flavorText: Seeing its swinging pendulum can induce sleep in three seconds, even in someone who just woke up.

name: Golem

attacks.name: Stone Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

attacks.name: Explosion
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself.

flavorText: It tumbles down mountains, leaving grooves from peak to base. Stay clear of these grooves.

name: Eiscue

abilities.name: Ice Face
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has full HP, it takes 60 less damage from your opponent's attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It drifted in on the flow of ocean waters from a frigid place. It keeps its head iced constantly to make sure it stays nice and cold.

abilities.name: Disrupting Spy
abilities.text: Once during your turn, when you put Chatot G from your hand onto your Bench, you may look at the top 4 cards of your opponent's deck. Put them back on top of your opponent's deck in any order.

attacks.name: Search and Escape
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Trainer card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Put Chatot G and all cards attached to it on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Swablu

attacks.name: Sing
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It constantly grooms its cotton-like wings. It takes a shower to clean itself if it becomes dirty.

name: Gliscor

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Sharp Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 40 more damage.

flavorText: It observes prey while hanging inverted from branches. When the chance presents itself, it swoops!

name: Grumpig

attacks.name: Psybeam
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Extrasensory
attacks.text: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does a strange dance to control foes' minds.

name: Cofagrigus

attacks.name: Chuck
attacks.text: Discard as many Pokémon Tool cards as you like from your hand. This attack does 40 damage times the number of cards you discarded.

attacks.name: Lock Up
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It has been said that they swallow those who get too close and turn them into mummies. They like to eat gold nuggets.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

name: Roselia

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: ROSELIA raised on clean drinking water are known to grow vividly colored flowers.

attacks.name: Long Tentacle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. If tails, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Toxic
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on the Defending Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Wild Kick
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Blaine's Doduo.

name: Houndour

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 more damage.

flavorText: To corner prey, they check each other's location using barks that only they can understand.

name: Burmy Sandy Cloak

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of an attack, including damage, done to Burmy Sandy Cloak during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

name: Tapu Koko-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Aero Trail
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may move any number of Lightning Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon. If you do, switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Tapu Thunder-GX
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Giratina ◇
rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.

abilities.name: Chaotic Star
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may attach 2 Psychic Energy cards from your hand to it.

attacks.name: Crisis Dive
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

name: Dracovish

abilities.name: Primal Law
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent can't play any Pokémon from their hand to evolve their Pokémon.

flavorText: Powerful legs and jaws made it the apex predator of its time. Its own overhunting of its prey was what drove it to extinction.

name: Turtonator-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Shell Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 8 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Discard 2 Fire Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Nitro Tank-GX
attacks.text: Attach 5 Fire Energy cards from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Ember
attacks.text: Discard a Fire Energy card attached to Bagon.

name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Sand Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It burrows and lives underground. If threatened, it curls itself up into a ball for protection.

name: Darkrai-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Dark Cloak
abilities.text: Each of your Pokémon that has any Darkness Energy attached to it has no Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Night Spear
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Slowking

attacks.name: Memory Melt
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand and put a card you find there in the Lost Zone.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon is so famed for its intellect that a proverb still persists in some regions: "When in doubt, ask Slowking."

name: Bellsprout

attacks.name: Inviting Scent
attacks.text: Switch the Defending Pokémon with 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Careless Tackle
attacks.text: Bellsprout does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Even though its body is extremely skinny, it is blindingly fast when catching its prey.

name: Electrike

ancientTrait.name: Ω Barrier
ancientTrait.text: Whenever your opponent plays a Trainer card (excluding Pokémon Tools and Stadium cards), prevent all effects of that card done to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Thunder Fang
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It stores static electricity in its fur for discharging. It gives off sparks if a storm approaches.

name: Spoink

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It bounces around on its tail to keep its heart pumping. It carries a pearl from Clamperl on its head.

name: Lairon

attacks.name: Mend
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for a Metal Energy card and attach it to Lairon. If you do, remove 2 damage counters from Lairon.

flavorText: For food, it digs up iron ore. It smashes its steely body against others to fight over territory.

name: Tentacruel

attacks.name: Void Tentacles
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Paranormal
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Ultra Beasts.

flavorText: It fires off ultrasonic waves from its red orbs to weaken its prey, and then it wraps them up in its 80 tentacles.

abilities.name: Buffer
abilities.text: If Hoppip would be Knocked Out by an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, Hoppip is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.

attacks.name: Miracle Powder
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Special Condition. The Defending Pokémon is now affected by that Special Condition.

name: Hoopa

attacks.name: Hyperspace Ring
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Item cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It gathers things it likes and passes them through its loop to teleport them to a secret place.

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Poison Boost
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 80 more damage. Then, remove that Special Condition from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It has a poison sac at its throat. When it croaks, the stored poison is churned for greater potency.

name: Tyranitar

abilities.name: Raging Roar
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may attach 1 Darkness Energy from your discard pile to this Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

attacks.name: Dark Mountain
attacks.text: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck. This attack does 50 more damage for each Supporter card discarded in this way.

flavorText: If it rampages, it knocks down mountains and buries rivers. Maps must be redrawn afterward.

name: Liepard

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Their beautiful form comes from the muscles they have developed. They run silently in the night.

name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Dual Draw
attacks.text: Each player draws 3 cards.

flavorText: It loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Ram
attacks.text: Rhydon does 20 damage to itself. If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon. Switch the Pokémon even if Rhydon is knocked out.)

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3600 degrees.

name: Mysterious Fossil
rules: Play Mysterious Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Mysterious Fossil counts as a Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Mysterious Fossil has no attacks, can't retreat, and can't be Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. If Mysterious Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Mysterious Fossil from play.

name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Charge Order
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage times the number of your Pawniard.

flavorText: Ignoring their injuries, groups attack by sinking the blades that cover their bodies into their prey.

name: Lapras

abilities.name: Go for a Swim
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and put them back in any order.

attacks.name: Ice Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: These Pokémon were once near extinction due to poaching. Following protective regulations, there is now an overabundance of them.

name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

name: Carbink

attacks.name: Crystal Barrier
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Wonder Blast
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Fairy Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Born from temperatures and pressures deep underground, it fires beams from the stone in its head.

name: Diglett

abilities.name: Submerge
abilities.text: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Dig Through
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It burrows through the ground at a shallow depth. It leaves raised earth in its wake, making it easy to spot.

name: Seaking
flavorText: In the autumn spawning season, they can be seen swimming powerfully up rivers and creeks.

name: Poipole

attacks.name: Spit Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Knockout Reviver
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is Knocked Out, your opponent can't take any Prize cards for it.

flavorText: This Ultra Beast is well enough liked to be chosen as a first partner in its own world.

name: Wishiwashi

abilities.name: Scatter
abilities.text: At the end of your opponent's turn, if this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, flip a coin. If tails, shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

flavorText: Wishiwashi assemble in this formation to face off against strong foes. It boasts a strength that earned it the name "demon of the sea."

name: Spiritomb

attacks.name: B Cancel
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from his or her hand to evolve the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: A Pokémon that was formed by 108 spirits. It is bound to a fissure in an odd keystone.

name: Grimer
flavorText: It was born from sludge on the ocean floor. In a sterile environment, the germs within its body can't multiply, and it dies.

name: Eiscue V
rules: V rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Cold Absorption
abilities.text: Whenever you attach a Water Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, heal 30 damage from it.

attacks.name: Blizzard
attacks.text: This attack also does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Smoochum
rules: If this Baby Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Psykiss
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a Special Energy card attached to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. Your opponent shuffles that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: It always rocks its head slowly backwards and forwards as if it is trying to kiss someone.

name: Chatot

attacks.name: Mimic
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw a number of cards equal to the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Chatter
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It can learn and speak human words. If they gather, they all learn the same saying.

name: Alolan Meowth

attacks.name: Nasty Plot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon was not originally found in Alola. Human actions caused a surge in their numbers, and they went feral. They're prideful and crafty.

name: Oricorio

attacks.name: Supernatural Dance
attacks.text: For each Pokémon in your opponent's discard pile, put 1 damage counter on your opponent's Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Revelation Dance
attacks.text: If there is no Stadium card in play, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: This Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. Its elegant, attractive dance will send the minds and hearts of its enemies to another world.

attacks.name: Water Plant
attacks.text: Search your discard pile for up to 2 Water Energy cards, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand.

attacks.name: Swift
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

name: Boldore

attacks.name: Hard Crash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage. If tails, this Pokémon does 20 damage to itself.

flavorText: Because its energy was too great to be contained, the energy leaked and formed orange crystals.

name: Manectric

attacks.name: Lightning Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Electric Shock
attacks.text: Discard all Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It discharges electricity from its mane. It creates a thundercloud overhead to drop lightning bolts.

name: Probopass

attacks.name: Stealth Rock
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 30 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: It freely controls three small units called Mini-Noses using magnetic force.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Wobbuffet from your hand onto Wynaut (this counts as evolving Wynaut), and remove all damage counters from Wynaut.

attacks.name: Alluring Smile
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon card or Evolution card for each Energy attached to Wynaut, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Tail Wag
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack Eevee during your opponent's next turn. (Benching either Pokémon ends this effect.)

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage; if tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: Its genetic code is irregular. It may mutate if it is exposed to radiation from elemental stones.

name: Rayquaza-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Celestial Roar
attacks.text: Discard the top 3 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Burst
attacks.text: Discard all basic Fire Energy or all basic Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage times the number of Energy you discarded.

name: Grumpig

attacks.name: Mirror Step
attacks.text: If 1 of your opponent's Pokémon in play has the same name as 1 of your Pokémon in play, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.

name: Seaking

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Seaking.

flavorText: It makes its nest by hollowing out boulders in streams with its horn. It defends its eggs with its life.

name: Ralts

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Feebas.

name: Croagunk

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at that card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: It rarely fights fairly, but that is strictly to ensure survival. It is popular as a mascot.

name: Pangoro

abilities.name: Benevolent Boss
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, it gets +20 HP for each of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Crazy Knuckle
attacks.text: If this Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: Although it possesses a violent temperament, it won't put up with bullying. It uses the leaf in its mouth to sense the movements of its enemies.

name: Hitmontop

attacks.name: Quick Draw
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Helicoptero
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It fights while spinning like a top. The centrifugal force boosts its destructive power by 10.

name: Blaine's Last Stand
rules: You can play this card only when it is the last card in your hand.
Draw 2 cards for each Fire Pokémon you have in play.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Staravia

attacks.name: Shot Air
attacks.text: Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It flies around forests and fields in search of bug Pokémon. It stays within a huge flock.

name: Lucario

abilities.name: Reflexive Retaliation
abilities.text: If this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 2 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

attacks.name: Aura Sphere
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: A well-trained one can sense auras to identify and take in the feelings of creatures over half a mile away.

name: Elgyem

attacks.name: First Contact
attacks.text: Search your deck for 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

flavorText: It uses its strong psychic power to squeeze its opponent's brain, causing unendurable headaches.

name: Rhyhorn

attacks.name: Dig Out
attacks.text: Discard the top card of your deck. If that card is a Fighting Energy, attach it to this Pokémon.

flavorText: Strong, but not too bright, this Pokémon can shatter even a skyscraper with its charging Tackles.

name: Raikou

abilities.name: Shining Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Lightning Energy attached to it, any damage done to this Pokémon by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Thunder Lance
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Lightning Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: This rough Pokémon stores energy inside its body, then sweeps across the land, shooting off electricity.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for Magikarp and put as many of them as you like onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Signs of Evolution
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Metapod and a Butterfree card, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: String Shot
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Eevee

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: A rare and unique Pokémon about which little is known, as few have been captured.

attacks.name: Smog
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Golem-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Megaton Fall
attacks.text: This Pokémon does 60 damage to itself.

name: Rotom Bike
rules: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand. Your turn ends.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

attacks.name: Naptime
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, remove 3 damage counters from Sabrina's Slowbro and Sabrina's Slowbro is now Asleep. If Sabrina's Slowbro has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

attacks.name: Screaming Headbutt
attacks.text: You can't use this attack during your next turn.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Deflector
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, whenever Chikorita takes damage, divide that damage in half (rounded down to the nearest 10). (Any other effects still happen.)

flavorText: A sweet aroma gently wafts from the leaf on its head. It is docile and loves to soak up the sun's rays.

name: Cherrim

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads. Cherrim is now Confused.

flavorText: If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun's rays with its whole body.

attacks.name: Flail
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Barboach.

name: Rattata
flavorText: Bites anything when it attacks. Small and very quick, it is a common sight in many places.

abilities.name: Submerge
abilities.text: As long as Mudkip is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to Mudkip by attacks (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Surprise
attacks.text: Choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

attacks.name: Acid
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

name: Kyurem

attacks.name: Call Forth Cold
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Water Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Hail Prison
attacks.text: Discard 2 Water Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: This legendary ice Pokémon waits for a hero to fill in the missing parts of its body with truth or ideals.

name: Remoraid

attacks.name: Water Arrow
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.

name: Grumpig

attacks.name: Magical Step
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused. Put 6 damage counters instead of 3 on the Confused Pokémon.

attacks.name: Grind
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to Grumpig.

flavorText: It uses black pearls to amplify its psycho-power. It does an odd dance to gain control over foes.

attacks.name: High Voltage
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent can't play Trainer cards from his or her hand during his or her next turn.

name: Aerodactyl
rules: Put this card onto your Bench only with the effect of Old Amber Aerodactyl

abilities.name: Ancient Scream
abilities.text: Your Pokémon's attacks do 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: A Pokémon that roamed the skies in the dinosaur era. Its teeth are like saw blades.

name: Golett

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Heal 40 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: The energy that burns inside it enables it to move, but no one has yet been able to identify this energy.

name: Magmar

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to Magmar.

flavorText: Born in the spout of a volcano, its body is covered by flames that shimmer like the sun.

name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Bronzor during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

name: Koffing

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its thin, filmy body is filled with gases that cause constant sniffles, coughs and teary eyes.

name: Sableye

attacks.name: Rummage
attacks.text: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck. Choose as many Trainer cards as you like, show them to your opponent, and put them into your hand. Put the other cards back on top of your deck. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Shadow Sneak
attacks.text: If you and your opponent have a different number of Prize cards left, this attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage.

flavorText: It hides in the darkness of caves. Its diet of gems has transformed its eyes into gemstones.

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Pikachu from your hand onto Pichu (this counts as evolving Pichu) and remove all damage counters from Pichu.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

attacks.name: Important Errands
attacks.text: Search your deck for a React Energy card, show it to your opponent, and put it into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Jump On
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

name: Reuniclus

abilities.name: Damage Swap
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.text: Does 10 more damage for each Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

flavorText: These remarkably intelligent Pokémon fight by controlling arms that can grip with rock-crushing power.

name: Unown [Z]
rules: You may have up to 4 Basic Pokémon cards in your deck with Unown in their names.

abilities.name: [Zoom]
abilities.text: As long as Unown [Z] is Benched, you pay no Energy cost to retreat a Pokémon with Unown in its name.

flavorText: It is believed that the variety of types of this Pokémon were created by evolutionary adaptation, as each possesses a different ability.

name: Cresselia

attacks.name: Lunar Payback
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

name: Hoothoot

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It always stands on one foot. It changes feet so fast, the movement can rarely be seen.

name: Raichu & Alolan Raichu-GX
rules: TAG TEAM rule: When your TAG TEAM is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tandem Shock
attacks.text: If this Pokémon was on the Bench and became your Active Pokémon this turn, this attack does 80 more damage, and your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Lightning Ride-GX
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If this Pokémon has at least 2 extra Lightning Energy attached to it (in addition to this attack's cost), this attack does 100 more damage. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Murkrow

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 card from your opponent's hand without looking. Look at the card you chose, then have your opponent shuffle that card into his or her deck.

flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

name: Venipede

attacks.name: Venoshock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: It discovers what is going on around it by using the feelers on its head and tail. It is brutally aggressive.

name: Salamence

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.text: Discard any Stadium card in play.

attacks.name: Power Howl
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage times the number of cards in your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Steam Blast
attacks.text: Discard 3 Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It's uncontrollable if enraged. It flies around spouting flames and scorching fields and mountains.

name: Salamence

abilities.name: Breakwing
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon, you may discard all Pokémon Tool cards attached to each of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Gaia Crush
attacks.text: Discard any Stadium card in play.

flavorText: As a result of its long-held dream of flying, its cellular structure changed, and wings grew out.

attacks.name: Teary Eyes
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Aron by attacks is reduced by 10.

attacks.name: Bursting Hand
attacks.text: Look at your opponent's hand. This attack does 10 damage times the number of Energy cards there.

attacks.name: Shuffle Attack
attacks.text: Look at a number of cards on top of your opponent's deck equal to the number of Energy cards attached to the Defending Pokémon. Put those cards in any order, and then put them back on top of your opponent's deck.

name: Alolan Ninetales-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Ice Blade
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Blizzard Edge
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ice Path-GX
attacks.text: Move all damage counters from this Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

abilities.name: Conductive Body
abilities.text: As long as Beldum is your Active Pokémon, you pay Colorless less to retreat Beldum for each Beldum on your Bench.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.text: Beldum does 10 damage to itself.

name: Skarmory

attacks.name: Air Crash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy attached to the Defending Pokémon.

attacks.name: Steel Wing
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Skarmory by attacks is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Despite being clad entirely in iron-hard armor, it flies at speeds over 180 mph.

name: Metapod

attacks.name: Stiffen
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Metapod during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

attacks.name: Stun Spore
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It is vulnerable to attack because its shell is soft, exposing its weak and tender body.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

name: Duskull

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: It loves the crying of children. It startles bad kids by passing through walls and making them cry.

name: Meowth

attacks.name: Act Tough
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached to it, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: Adores round objects. It wanders the streets on a nightly basis to look for dropped loose change.

name: Xatu

attacks.name: Energy Cycle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose 1 Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon and 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. Attach that Energy card to that Pokémon.

flavorText: In South America, it is said that its right eye sees the future and its left eye views the past.

name: Espeon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Miraculous Shine
attacks.text: Devolve each of your opponent's evolved Pokémon and put the highest Stage Evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Karate Chop
attacks.text: Does 80 damage minus 10 damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It grows angry if you see its eyes and gets angrier if you run. If you beat it, it gets even madder.

name: Magby
rules: If your Active Pokémon is a Baby Pokémon and your opponent announces an attack, your opponent flips a coin (before doing anything else). If tails, your opponent's turn ends.

attacks.name: Energy Catch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put a basic Energy card from your discard pile into your hand.

name: Primeape

attacks.name: Lucha Fight
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 30 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: It has been known to become so angry that it dies as a result. Its face looks peaceful in death, however.

name: Arctovish

attacks.name: Hard Face
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Cold Breath
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Though it's able to capture prey by freezing its surroundings, it has trouble eating the prey afterward because its mouth is on top of its head.

name: Gengar

abilities.name: Life Shaker
abilities.text: As often as you like during your turn, you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Psychic Pokémon to another of your Psychic Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hypnoblast
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: On the night of a full moon, if shadows move on their own and laugh, it must be Gengar's doing.

attacks.name: Stoke
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Fire Energy card and attach it to Blaine's Growlithe. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Body Slam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Primal Breath
attacks.text: Your opponent can't play any Pokémon from his or her hand to Evolve or to Level-Up the Defending Pokémon during his or her next turn.

name: Galvantula

attacks.name: Stun Needle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Shocking Pursuit
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: It launches electrified fur from its abdomen as its means of attack. Opponents hit by the fur could be in for three full days and nights of paralysis.

name: Alolan Grimer

attacks.name: Super Poison Breath
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The crystals on Grimer's body are lumps of toxins. If one falls off, lethal poisons leak out.

attacks.name: Sudden Growth
attacks.text: Attach any number of basic Energy cards from your hand to Politoed.

attacks.name: Frog Hop
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 damage plus 20 more damage.

attacks.name: Energy Splash
attacks.text: Move 2 Water Energy cards attached to Politoed to 1 or 2 of your Benched Pokémon. (You may put both on the same Pokémon or 1 each on 2 different Pokémon.) If you have no Benched Pokémon, ignore this effect.

name: Chewtle
flavorText: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

name: Vullaby

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their wings are too tiny to allow them to fly. They guard their posteriors with bones that were gathered by Mandibuzz.

attacks.name: Night Attack
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Toxicroak

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: The croaking that Toxicroak produces before a battle is for churning the poison it has stored in its poison sac.

name: Lt. Surge's Strategy
rules: You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.
During this turn, you can play 3 Supporter cards (including this card).
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

name: Chimchar

attacks.name: Lunge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: Its fiery rear end is fueled by gas made in its belly. Even rain can't extinguish the fire.

name: Hitmonlee

attacks.name: Stretch Kick
attacks.text: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: The legs freely contract and stretch. The stretchy legs allow it to hit a distant foe with a rising kick.

name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Water Splash
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.

flavorText: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

name: Snubbull

attacks.name: Raging Headbutt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Snubbull. If tails, this attack does 10 damage.

flavorText: It has an active, playful nature. Many people like to frolic with it because of its affectionate ways.

attacks.name: Sleep Powder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Houndoom

abilities.name: Revenge Fang
abilities.text: If you have less Benched Pokémon than your opponent, each of Houndoom's attacks does 40 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Burning Sensation
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, the Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

attacks.name: Bright Flame
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, discard 2 Fire Energy attached to Houndoom.

flavorText: Long ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.

name: Pikachu

attacks.name: Meal Time
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, draw a card.

flavorText: Its nature is to store up electricity. Forests where nests of Pikachu live are dangerous, since the trees are so often struck by lightning.

attacks.name: Rage
attacks.text: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each damage counter on Cubone.

name: Persian

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: Its lithe muscles allow it to walk without making a sound. It attacks in an instant.

name: Whirlipede

attacks.name: Spin Turn
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Protected by a hard shell, it spins its body like a wheel and crashes furiously into its enemies.

name: Timburr
flavorText: It fights by swinging a piece of timber around. It is close to evolving when it can handle the lumber without difficulty.

attacks.name: Pranks
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, choose a card from your opponent's discard pile and put it on top of his or her deck.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Articuno-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Legendary Ascent
abilities.text: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon. If you do, move any number of Water Energy from your other Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Cold Crush-GX
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from both Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Magikarp
flavorText: For no reason, it jumps and splashes about, making it easy for predators like Pidgeotto to catch it mid-jump.

name: Delibird

attacks.name: Happy Delivery
attacks.text: Choose any number of your Benched Pokémon that don't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to them. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a Pokémon Tool card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It has a generous habit of sharing its food with people and Pokémon, so it's always scrounging around for more food.

name: Murkrow
flavorText: It is said that when chased, it lures its attacker onto dark mountain trails where the foe will get lost.

name: Kyogre

attacks.name: Spring Tides
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

attacks.name: Ocean Cyclone
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

name: Seel
flavorText: The protruding horn on its head is very hard. This horn is used for bashing through thick ice.

name: Cubone

attacks.name: Leer
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it thinks of its deceased mother, it weeps loudly. Mandibuzz that hear its cries will attack it from the air.

attacks.name: Shed Skin
attacks.text: Remove 4 damage counters from Seviper.

attacks.name: Bite and Escape
attacks.text: You may switch Seviper with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Paralyze Poison
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Sleepy Ball
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

name: Shelgon

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Shelgon's Protect Charge attack's base damage is 80.

attacks.name: Protect Charge
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Shelgon by attacks is reduced by 30 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: Within its rugged shell, its cells have begun changing. The shell peels off the instant it evolves.

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Larvitar.

name: Voltorb

attacks.name: Continuous Tumble
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Usually found in power plants. Easily mistaken for a Poké Ball, it has zapped many people.

name: Froakie

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It secretes flexible bubbles from its chest and back. The bubbles reduce the damage it would otherwise take when attacked.

name: Wishiwashi-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: School Storm
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Wishiwashi and Wishiwashi-GX in play.

attacks.name: Massive Catch-GX
attacks.text: Look at the top 12 cards of your deck and put any number of Basic Pokémon you find there onto your Bench. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Drednaw

attacks.name: Vise Bite
attacks.text: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Jaw Lock
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

flavorText: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

name: Riolu

attacks.name: Double Chop
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: They communicate with one another using their auras. They are able to run all through the night.

attacks.name: Kindle
attacks.text: Discard 1 Energy card attached to Blaine's Charmander in order to use this attack. If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

attacks.name: Nap
attacks.text: Remove 2 damage counters from Mudkip.

attacks.name: Mach Blow
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon is a Pokémon SP, this attack's base damage is 80 instead of 20.

attacks.name: Giant Tail
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

name: Chimchar
rules: Cheri Berry: If Chimchar is Paralyzed, remove the Special Condition Paralyzed from Chimchar at the end of each player's turn.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Retaliate
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Mach Claw
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Bisharp pursues prey in the company of a large group of Pawniard. Then Bisharp finishes off the prey.

name: Golurk

attacks.name: Devolution Punch
attacks.text: Devolve the Defending Pokémon and put the highest Stage evolution card on it into your opponent's hand.

attacks.name: Ghost Hammer
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon has no Weakness.

flavorText: It flies around the sky at Mach speeds. Removing the seal on its chest makes its internal energy go out of control.

name: Latios-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Raid
attacks.text: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn.

attacks.name: Light Pulse
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of your opponent's attacks, except damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Recharge
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to Electrike. Shuffle your deck afterward.

attacks.name: Quick Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage plus 20 more damage.

abilities.name: Strange Spiral
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Poliwrath is your Active Pokémon, you may discard a basic Energy card attached to Poliwrath. If you do, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused. This power can't be used if Poliwrath is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Spiral Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

name: Carvanha

attacks.name: Focus Energy
attacks.text: During your next turn, Carvanha's Bite attack's base damage is 40.

flavorText: They form packs to attack boats and rip out their hulls to sink them. They live in rivers in the jungle.

name: Gigalith

attacks.name: Shear
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Fighting Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rock Bullet
attacks.text: Does 20 more damage for each Fighting Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: The solar energy absorbed by its body's orange crystals is magnified internally and fired from its mouth.

name: M Charizard-EX
rules: Mega Evolution rule: When 1 of your Pokémon becomes a Mega Evolution Pokémon, your turn ends.
Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Wild Blaze
attacks.text: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck.

name: Beheeyem

attacks.name: Synchronoise
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon that shares a type with the Defending Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It uses psychic power to control an opponent's brain and tamper with its memories.

attacks.name: Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Cyndaquil

attacks.name: Smokescreen
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It is very meek and constantly crouches in a defensive posture. When threatened, it protects itself with the flame on its back.

name: Hippopotas

attacks.name: Sand Jet
attacks.text: Does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It shuts its nostrils tight then travels through sand as if walking. They form colonies of around ten.

name: Masquerain

attacks.name: Centrifugal Force
attacks.text: Does 20 damage times the number of Colorless Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).

attacks.name: Swirling Ripple
attacks.text: If your opponent has any Water Pokémon in play, this attack does 30 damage plus 30 more damage and the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Its antennae have eye patterns on them. Its four wings enable it to hover and fly in any direction.

name: Dusclops

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into his or her deck.

flavorText: It seeks drifting will-o'-the-wisps and sucks them into its empty body. What happens inside is a mystery.

name: Tynamo

abilities.name: Levitate
abilities.text: If this Pokémon has any Energy attached, it has no Retreat Cost.

flavorText: One alone can emit only trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

name: Ekans

attacks.name: Poison Sting
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

flavorText: A carnivore that swallows its prey whole. Pidgeys and Spearows are its favorite food.

name: Slowbro

attacks.name: Amnesia
attacks.text: Choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon's attacks. That Pokémon can't use that attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Though usually dim witted, it seems to become inspired if the SHELLDER on its tail bites down.

name: Nidorina

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Double Kick
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The female's horn develops slowly. Prefers physical attacks such as clawing and biting.

name: Hoppip

attacks.name: Bounce
attacks.text: You may switch Hoppip with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: To keep from being blown away by the wind, they gather in clusters. But they do enjoy gentle breezes.

name: Onix

attacks.name: Swing Around
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: Opening its large mouth, it ingests massive amounts of soil and creates long tunnels.

name: Staryu

attacks.name: Bubblebeam
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: At night, the center of its body slowly flickers with the same rhythm as a human heartbeat.

attacks.name: Reckless Charge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Carvanha does 10 damage to itself.

name: Exeggcute

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Leech Seed
attacks.text: Unless all damage from this attack is prevented, you may remove 1 damage counter from Exeggcute.

flavorText: Often mistaken for eggs. When disturbed, they quickly gather and attack in swarms.

name: Lunatone

abilities.name: Sol Shade
abilities.text: If you have Solrock in play, Fire Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) have no Abilities, except Pokémon-GX and Pokémon-EX.

attacks.name: Psyshock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

name: Woobat

attacks.name: Dual Cut
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: The heart-shaped mark left on a body after a Woobat has been attached to it is said to bring good fortune.

name: Breloom

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.text: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sky Uppercut
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: Its short arms stretch when it throws punches. Its technique is equal to that of pro boxers.

name: Hitmonchan

attacks.name: Bullet Punch
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.

flavorText: The arm-twisting punches it throws pulverize even concrete. It rests after three minutes of fighting.

name: Dragon Talon
rules: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn't already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.
If the Dragon Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if that Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 3 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

name: Naganadel-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Beast Raid
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each of your Ultra Beasts in play.

attacks.name: Jet Needle
attacks.text: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness or Resistance.

attacks.name: Stinger-GX
attacks.text: Both players shuffle their Prize cards into their decks. Then, each player puts the top 3 cards of their deck face down as their Prize cards. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Caterpie

attacks.name: Spin Tackle
attacks.text: Caterpie does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: For protection, it releases a horrible stench from the antennae on its head to drive away enemies.

name: Muk

abilities.name: Poison Sacs
abilities.text: The Special Condition Poisoned is not removed when your opponent's Pokémon evolve or devolve.

attacks.name: Toxic Secretion
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 2 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

flavorText: Their food sources have decreased, and their numbers have declined sharply. Sludge ponds are being built to prevent their extinction.

attacks.name: Tail Code
attacks.text: Move an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Snap Attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, this attack's base damage is 20 instead of 60.

name: Sandshrew

attacks.name: Sand-attack
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: Burrows deep underground in arid locations far from water. It only emerges to hunt for food.

name: Rotom Phone
rules: Look at the top 5 cards of your deck, choose 1 of them, and shuffle the other cards back into your deck. Then, put the card you chose on top of your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

abilities.name: Soothing Aroma
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may flip a coin. If heads, remove 1 damage counter from each of your Pokémon that has any. This power can't be used if Meganium is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Poisonpowder
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

name: Eelektrik

attacks.name: Buzz Flip
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

name: Groudon-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Tromp
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Giant Claw
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon already has 2 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 40 more damage.

name: Misty's Cerulean City Gym
rules: The attacks of Starmie-GX (both yours and your opponent's) do 40 more damage to the opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can't play this card.

abilities.name: Healing Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may remove 1 damage counter from each of your Active Pokémon. This power can't be used if Xatu is affected by a Special Condition.

attacks.name: Psyimpact
attacks.text: Put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Pokémon.

name: Cosmog

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

name: Golisopod

abilities.name: Armor
abilities.text: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Resolute Claws
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Pokémon-GX or a Pokémon-EX, this attack does 70 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: With a flashing slash of its giant sharp claws, it cleaves seawater—or even air—right in two.

name: Kirlia

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: If its Trainer becomes happy, it overflows with energy, dancing joyously while spinning about.

name: Mandibuzz

attacks.name: Bone Drop
attacks.text: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon that has an Ability. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Watching from the sky, they swoop to strike weakened Pokémon on the ground. They decorate themselves with bones.

attacks.name: Smash Punch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

attacks.name: Submission
attacks.text: Machop does 10 damage to itself.

name: Chikorita

attacks.name: Growl
attacks.text: If the Defending Pokémon attacks Chikorita during your opponent's next turn, any damage done to Chikorita is reduced by 10 (before applying Weakness and Resistance). (Benching or evolving either Pokémon ends this effect.)

flavorText: Its pleasantly aromatic leaf has the ability to check the humidity and temperature.

name: Mareep
flavorText: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You'll want to pet it because it's cute, but if you use your bare hand, you'll get a painful shock.

name: Machamp

attacks.name: Macho Revenge
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage for each Fighting Pokémon in your discard pile.

attacks.name: Dynamite Punch
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

flavorText: It quickly swings its four arms to rock its opponents with ceaseless punches and chops from all angles.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Burned.

name: Dugtrio

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.text: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: In battle, it digs through the ground and strikes the unsuspecting foe from an unexpected direction.

name: Silcoon

attacks.name: Harden
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, If Silcoon would be damaged by an attack, prevent that attack's damage done to Silcoon if that damage is 30 or less.

attacks.name: Entangling String
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It anchors itself by wrapping twigs with the silk from its body. It motionlessly awaits evolution.

name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Revenge
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during his or her last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

attacks.name: Thunder Blast
attacks.text: Discard an Energy attached to this Pokémon.

flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

name: Electrike

attacks.name: Thunder Jolt
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, Electrike does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: Using electricity stored in its fur, it stimulates its muscles to heighten its reaction speed.

name: Toxapex-GX
rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Spike Cannon
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Super Intense Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 10 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.

attacks.name: Total Shelter-GX
attacks.text: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

name: Skorupi

attacks.name: Pin Missile
attacks.text: Flip 4 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

flavorText: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

name: Dialga-EX
rules: Pokémon-EX rule: When a Pokémon-EX has been Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Reverse Edge
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Fast Forward
attacks.text: For each Plasma Energy attached to this Pokémon, discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Earth Rift
attacks.text: This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness or Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Then, flip a coin. If tails, this attack can't be used during your next turn.

attacks.name: Iron Smash
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both are heads, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage. If both are tails, this attack does nothing. If 1 is heads and 1 is tails, this attack just does 50 damage.

name: Rhydon

attacks.name: Mad Mountain
attacks.text: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, discard the top card of your opponent's deck for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: Protected by an armor-like hide, it is capable of living in molten lava of 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

name: Purrloin

attacks.name: Fake Out
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Its cute act is a ruse. When victims let down their guard, they find their items taken. It attacks with sharp claws.

name: Alolan Muk

abilities.name: Power of Alchemy
abilities.text: Each Basic Pokémon in play, in each player's hand, and in each player's discard pile has no Abilities.

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: While it's unexpectedly quiet and friendly, if it's not fed any trash for a while, it will smash its Trainer's furnishings and eat up the fragments.

name: Bagon

attacks.name: Scary Face
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon can't attack or retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Dreaming of one day flying, it practices by leaping off cliffs every day.

name: Scrafty

attacks.name: Dangerous Head
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, this attack does 50 more damage.

flavorText: It pulls up its shed skin to protect itself while it kicks. The bigger the crest, the more respected it is.

name: Altaria

abilities.name: Fight Song
abilities.text: Your Dragon Pokémon's attacks do 20 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: If it bonds with a person, it will gently envelop the friend with its soft wings, then hum.

attacks.name: Thundershock
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Blaze
attacks.text: Does 10 damage to each Grass Pokémon on your opponent's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

name: Lanturn

attacks.name: Rushing Water
attacks.text: Move an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon to another of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Confuse Ray
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Lanturn's light can shine up from great depths. It is nicknamed "The Deep-Sea Star."

name: Chingling

abilities.name: Baby Evolution
abilities.text: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may put Chimecho from your hand onto Chingling (this counts as evolving Chingling) and remove all damage counters from Chingling.

attacks.name: Uproar
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: It emits cries by agitating an orb at the back of its throat. It moves with flouncing hops.

name: Dhelmise

abilities.name: Steelworker
abilities.text: Your Metal Pokémon's attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Anchor Shot
attacks.text: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Swinging its massive anchor, it can KO Wailord in a single blow. What appears to be green seaweed is actually its body.

name: Mimikyu

attacks.name: Filch
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

attacks.name: Copycat
attacks.text: If your opponent's Pokémon used an attack that isn't a GX attack during their last turn, use it as this attack.

flavorText: Its actual appearance is unknown. A scholar who saw what was under its rag was overwhelmed by horror and died of the shock.

name: Yveltal

attacks.name: Oblivion Wing
attacks.text: Attach a Darkness Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Darkness Blade
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.
